  • Pick the modal by meaning: ability, obligation, possibility…

ru.title: Система модальных
ru.html: Модальные (can, could, must, have to, should, might, may, will, would) добавляют смысл: способность, обязанность, совет, возможность. У всех одни правила.

kk.title: Модаль жүйесі
kk.html: Модальдер (can, could, must, have to, should, might, may, will, would) етістікке мағына қосады: қабілет, міндет, кеңес, мүмкіндік. Барлығы бірдей грамматикамен жүреді.

ru.title: Три золотых правила
ru.html: 1) Без -s (she can). 2) Обычно без to (must go; но have to go). 3) Вопрос/отрицание без do (Can you…? / can't).

kk.title: Үш алтын ереже
kk.html: 1) -s жоқ (she can). 2) Көбіне to жоқ (must go; бірақ have to go). 3) Сұрақ/болымсызда do жоқ (Can you…? / can't).

ru.title: Карта значений
ru.html: способность: can/could; обязанность: must/have to/should; разрешение/просьба: can/could/may; возможность: might/may/could; уверенность: must (да) / can't (нет).

kk.title: Мағына картасы
kk.html: қабілет: can/could; міндет: must/have to/should; рұқсат/өтініш: can/could/may; мүмкіндік: might/may/could; сенімділік: must (иә) / can't (жоқ).

ru.title: Всё вместе
ru.html: «Could you help me? I can't open this.» — «You should push. It might be stuck.»

kk.title: Бәрі бірге
kk.html: «Could you help me? I can't open this.» — «You should push. It might be stuck.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: He can plays → He can play. Do you should go? → Should you go? She must to leave → She must leave.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: He can plays → He can play. Do you should go? → Should you go? She must to leave → She must leave.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Модальный + базовый глагол; без -s, без do. 'to' только с have to / ought to. Модальный выбирайте по смыслу.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Модаль + бастапқы етістік; -s жоқ, do жоқ. 'to' тек have to / ought to-мен. Модальді мағынасына қарай таңдаңыз.

activities.before: I don't know the answer. It

activities.why: no 'do' with modals: “Can you come tonight?”

activities.prompt: Match each modal to its meaning.

steps.npc: Can you help me with this report?

options.t: Sure. You should save it first — it might crash.
Sure. You should to save it first.

activities.why: Modal + base verb; choose the modal by the meaning you need.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Most nouns add -s to make the plural (more than one). Some follow special spelling rules, and a few are irregular.

one book → two books · one box → two boxes · one child → two children

learn.title: Spelling rules
learn.html:
most nouns + scat → cats, house → houses
-s/-sh/-ch/-x + esbus → buses, watch → watches
consonant + y → iesbaby → babies, city → cities
-f/-fe → vesknife → knives, leaf → leaves

learn.title: Irregular plurals
learn.html:
man → menwoman → women
child → childrenperson → people
foot → feettooth → teeth
no changesheep → sheep, fish → fish

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
How many children do they have?
Three — two boys and a girl. And two dogs!
Busy house! Do the kids brush their teeth alone?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
two childs  ✓ two children.
✗
three womans  ✓ three women.
✗
some informations  ✓ some information. (uncountable — no plural)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Most plurals add -s; -s/sh/ch/x → -es; consonant+y → -ies.
  • -f/-fe often → -ves (knife→knives).
  • Learn the irregulars: men, women, children, people, feet, teeth.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Большинство существительных во множественном добавляют -s. У некоторых особое написание, а несколько неправильных: book → books, box → boxes, child → children.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп зат есім көпше түрде -s қосады. Кейбірінде арнайы емле, бірнешеуі бұрыс: one book → books, one box → boxes, one child → children.

ru.title: Правила написания
ru.html: Обычно +s (cats). -s/sh/ch/x → +es (buses, watches). Согласный+y → -ies (babies). -f/-fe → -ves (knives).

kk.title: Емле ережелері
kk.html: Көбіне +s (cats). -s/sh/ch/x → +es (buses, watches). Дауыссыз+y → -ies (babies). -f/-fe → -ves (knives).

ru.title: Неправильные множественные
ru.html: man→men, woman→women, child→children, person→people, foot→feet, tooth→teeth. Без изменения: sheep, fish.

kk.title: Бұрыс көпшелер
kk.html: man→men, woman→women, child→children, person→people, foot→feet, tooth→teeth. Өзгермейтіндер: sheep, fish.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «How many children do they have?» — «Three — two boys and a girl. And two dogs!»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «How many children do they have?» — «Three — two boys and a girl. And two dogs!»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: two childs → two children. three womans → three women. informations → information (неисчисляемое).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: two childs → two children. three womans → three women. informations → information (саналмайтын).

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Обычно -s; -s/sh/ch/x → -es; согласный+y → -ies. -f/-fe часто → -ves. Учите неправильные: men, women, children, people, feet, teeth.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Көбіне -s; -s/sh/ch/x → -es; дауыссыз+y → -ies. -f/-fe жиі → -ves. Бұрыстарды жаттаңыз: men, women, children, people, feet, teeth.

activities.prompt: Sort each noun by how it forms its plural.
Match each singular to its plural.

activities.after: were waiting at the bus stop. (woman → plural)

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Countable nouns can be counted (one apple, two apples). Uncountable nouns cannot (water, money, rice) — no plural, no a/an.

an apple · some water

learn.title: a/an, some, any
learn.html:
a / anone countable thing: a book, an egg
some+ / plural or uncountable: some apples, some milk
any− / ? plural or uncountable: any money? not any eggs

learn.title: some vs any
learn.html:
some (+ statements)any (− and ?)
I've got some bread.I haven't got any bread.
There are some eggs.Are there any eggs?
⭐
Use some in offers/requests: Would you like some tea? Can I have some water?

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Is there any milk?
There's some in the fridge. We need an egg, too.
And we haven't got any bread.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a water  ✓ some water. (uncountable)
✗
I don't have some money.  ✓ I don't have any money.
✗
an information  ✓ some information.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• a/an = one countable thing.
  • some = positive (plural/uncountable); any = negatives & questions.
  • Uncountable nouns take no a/an and no plural.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Исчисляемые считаются (one apple, two apples). Неисчисляемые — нет (water, money, rice): нет множественного, нет a/an: an apple · some water.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Саналатын зат есімдер саналады (one apple, two apples). Саналмайтындар саналмайды (water, money, rice) — көпше жоқ, a/an жоқ: an apple · some water.

ru.title: a/an, some, any
ru.html: a/an — один исчисляемый (a book, an egg). some — утверждение, множ./неисчисл. (some apples, some milk). any — отрицание/вопрос (any money? not any eggs).

kk.title: a/an, some, any
kk.html: a/an — бір саналатын зат (a book, an egg). some — болымды, көпше/саналмайтын (some apples, some milk). any — болымсыз/сұрақ (any money? not any eggs).

ru.title: some и any
ru.html: some — в утверждении (I've got some bread). any — в отрицании/вопросе (Are there any eggs?). В предложении/просьбе — some: Would you like some tea?

kk.title: some мен any
kk.html: some — болымды сөйлемде (I've got some bread). any — болымсыз/сұрақта (Are there any eggs?). Ұсыныс/өтініште — some: Would you like some tea?

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Is there any milk?» — «There's some in the fridge. We need an egg, too.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Is there any milk?» — «There's some in the fridge. We need an egg, too.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a water → some water. don't have some money → don't have any money. an information → some information.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a water → some water. don't have some money → don't have any money. an information → some information.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: a/an = один исчисляемый. some = утверждение (множ./неисчисл.); any = отрицание/вопрос. У неисчисляемых нет a/an и множественного.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: a/an = бір саналатын зат. some = болымды (көпше/саналмайтын); any = болымсыз/сұрақ. Саналмайтындарда a/an мен көпше жоқ.

activities.why: Uncountable nouns take some, not a/an.
apple starts with a vowel sound → an.
chair/idea/orange are countable; rice/money/bread are not.

options.t: No, there isn't any. And we need some milk.
No, there isn't some. And we need any milk.
options.why: negative → any; positive → some: “isn't any… need some milk.”

activities.why: a/an for one countable thing; some in positives; any in negatives and questions.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some nouns can be both countable and uncountable, with a small change in meaning.

some coffee (the drink, general) · a coffee (one cup, in a café)

learn.title: General vs a portion
learn.html:
uncountable (general)countable (a unit/portion)
I like coffee.Can I have a coffee? (a cup)
Do you want ice cream?I bought two ice creams. (two portions)

learn.title: Containers & units
learn.html: To count uncountable things, we use a unit word: a glass of water, a piece of paper, a bottle of milk, a slice of bread.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Would you like a coffee?
Yes please, and a glass of water.
Sure. Do you take sugar?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'd like two waters bottles.  ✓ two bottles of water.
✗
a paper (= material)  ✓ a piece of paper. ('a paper' = a newspaper)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Same noun can be uncountable (general) or countable (a portion/cup).
  • Count uncountables with unit words: a glass of, a piece of.
  • a coffee = a cup of coffee.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые существительные бывают и теми, и другими с небольшой сменой смысла: some coffee (напиток, вообще) · a coffee (одна чашка, в кафе).

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір зат есімдер екеуі де бола алады — мағынасы аздап өзгереді: some coffee (сусын, жалпы) · a coffee (бір кесе, кафеде).

ru.title: Общее и порция
ru.html: Неисчисл. (вообще): I like coffee. Исчисл. (порция/чашка): Can I have a coffee? two ice creams = две порции.

kk.title: Жалпы мен бір бөлік
kk.html: Саналмайтын (жалпы): I like coffee. Саналатын (бір бөлік/кесе): Can I have a coffee? two ice creams = екі порция.

ru.title: Ёмкости и единицы
ru.html: Чтобы посчитать неисчисляемое, берём слово-меру: a glass of water, a piece of paper, a bottle of milk, a slice of bread.

kk.title: Ыдыс пен өлшем сөздері
kk.html: Саналмайтынды санау үшін өлшем сөзі: a glass of water, a piece of paper, a bottle of milk, a slice of bread.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Would you like a coffee?» — «Yes please, and a glass of water.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Would you like a coffee?» — «Yes please, and a glass of water.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: two waters bottles → two bottles of water. a paper (в значении материала) → a piece of paper.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: two waters bottles → two bottles of water. a paper (материал мағынасында) → a piece of paper.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Одно существительное — неисчисл. (вообще) или исчисл. (порция/чашка). Считаем словами-мерами: a glass of, a piece of. a coffee = чашка кофе.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Бір зат есім саналмайтын (жалпы) не саналатын (бөлік/кесе) болуы мүмкін. Өлшем сөздерімен санаңыз: a glass of, a piece of. a coffee = бір кесе кофе.

activities.q: “Two coffees, please.” In a café this means…
why: Correct — a coffee = a cup of coffee.

activities.why: Use unit words; 'a paper' means a newspaper.

activities.prompt: Match each noun to its usual unit word.
activities.why: glass of water, piece of paper, bottle of milk, loaf of bread.

activities.q: At a café: “Can I have ___, please?”

activities.instruction: Make it countable with a unit word:
activities.answer: i need two pieces of paper
alts: i need two pieces of paper.

options.t: Two coffees and a bottle of water, please.
options.why: a coffee = a cup; bottle of water for the uncountable.

options.t: Two coffee and a water bottle, please.

activities.why: a coffee = a cup; count uncountables with unit words (glass/piece/bottle of).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
many goes with countable nouns; much goes with uncountable nouns. Both mean "a large amount".

How many people? · How much money?

learn.title: When to use them
learn.html:
many + countable pluralHow many eggs? · not many cars
much + uncountableHow much water? · not much time
a lot of + both (positive)a lot of friends · a lot of rain
⭐
much/many are most common in questions and negatives; in positive sentences we usually say a lot of.

learn.title: How much / How many
learn.html:
Ask about amount: How many + countable, How much + uncountable.

How many brothers have you got? · How much does it cost?

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
How much milk do we need?
Not much — one carton. But we need a lot of eggs.
How many?
About twelve.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
How much people?  ✓ How many people?
✗
much friends  ✓ many friends / a lot of friends.
✗
How many money?  ✓ How much money?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• many + countable; much + uncountable.
  • a lot of works with both (esp. positive sentences).
  • How many …? / How much …?

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: many — с исчисляемыми; much — с неисчисляемыми. Оба означают «много»: How many people? · How much money?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: many — саналатын зат есіммен; much — саналмайтынмен. Екеуі де «көп мөлшер» дегенді білдіреді: How many people? · How much money?

ru.title: Когда использовать
ru.html: many + исчисл. множ. (many eggs). much + неисчисл. (much water). a lot of — с обоими, особенно в утверждениях.

kk.title: Қашан қолданамыз
kk.html: many + саналатын көпше (many eggs). much + саналмайтын (much water). a lot of — екеуіне де, әсіресе болымды сөйлемде.

ru.title: How much / How many
ru.html: Спросить о количестве: How many + исчисл., How much + неисчисл.: How many brothers…? How much does it cost?

kk.title: How much / How many
kk.html: Мөлшерді сұрау: How many + саналатын, How much + саналмайтын: How many brothers…? How much does it cost?

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «How much milk do we need?» — «Not much. But we need a lot of eggs.» — «How many?»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «How much milk do we need?» — «Not much. But we need a lot of eggs.» — «How many?»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: How much people? → How many people? much friends → many/a lot of friends. How many money? → How much money?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: How much people? → How many people? much friends → many/a lot of friends. How many money? → How much money?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: many + исчисл.; much + неисчисл. a lot of — с обоими (особ. в утверждении). How many…? / How much…?

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: many + саналатын; much + саналмайтын. a lot of — екеуіне (әсіресе болымды). How many…? / How much…?

activities.prompt: Which quantifier goes with each noun?

steps.npc: How much flour do we need?

options.t: Not much — and we don't need many eggs either.
Not many — and we don't need much eggs.

activities.why: many + countable, much + uncountable; a lot of for positive statements.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
too much / too many = more than you want (a problem). enough = the right amount. not enough = less than you need.

too much noise · not enough chairs

learn.title: Which one?
learn.html:
too much + uncountabletoo much sugar, too much traffic
too many + countabletoo many people, too many emails
enough + nounenough money, enough seats

learn.title: Position of enough
learn.html:
enough + nounadjective + enough
enough moneybig enough, old enough
⭐
enough comes before a noun but after an adjective: "not warm enough".

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
This café is too busy — too many people.
And too much noise. Is there enough room outside?
Just about. The table's big enough for us.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
too many traffic  ✓ too much traffic. (uncountable)
✗
enough big  ✓ big enough. (after adjective)
✗
money enough  ✓ enough money. (before noun)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• too much + uncountable; too many + countable (a problem).
  • enough = the right amount; not enough = too little.
  • enough + noun, but adjective + enough.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: too much / too many = больше, чем нужно (проблема). enough = достаточно. not enough = меньше, чем нужно: too much noise · not enough chairs.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: too much / too many = қалағаннан көп (мәселе). enough = жеткілікті мөлшер. not enough = қажеттен аз: too much noise · not enough chairs.

ru.title: Какой из них?
ru.html: too much + неисчисл. (too much sugar). too many + исчисл. (too many people). enough + сущ. (enough money).

kk.title: Қайсысы?
kk.html: too much + саналмайтын (too much sugar). too many + саналатын (too many people). enough + зат есім (enough money).

ru.title: Место enough
ru.html: enough стоит перед существительным, но после прилагательного: enough money; big enough; not warm enough.

kk.title: enough орны
kk.html: enough зат есімнің алдында, бірақ сын есімнің соңында: enough money; big enough; not warm enough.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «This café is too busy — too many people.» — «And too much noise. Is there enough room outside?»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «This café is too busy — too many people.» — «And too much noise. Is there enough room outside?»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: too many traffic → too much traffic. enough big → big enough. money enough → enough money.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: too many traffic → too much traffic. enough big → big enough. money enough → enough money.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: too much + неисчисл.; too many + исчисл. (проблема). enough = достаточно; not enough = мало. enough + сущ., но прилаг. + enough.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: too much + саналмайтын; too many + саналатын (мәселе). enough = жеткілікті; not enough = аз. enough + зат есім, бірақ сын есім + enough.

activities.before: We can't all sit down. There aren't

activities.instruction: Say it's a problem of amount:
activities.prompt: The box is small. It can't hold everything.
alts: the box is not big enough

options.t: No — too many people and not enough tables.
options.why: too many + countable; not enough + noun.

options.t: No — too much people and not enough big tables.

activities.why: too much/too many = a problem of amount; enough = the right amount, placed before nouns but after adjectives.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
English has cardinal numbers for counting (one, two, three) and ordinal numbers for order (first, second, third).

I have two sisters. · It's her second birthday.

learn.title: Ordinals
learn.html:
1st first2nd second
3rd third4th fourth
5th fifth8th eighth
9th ninth12th twelfth

Most add -th; 20th = twentieth. Dates use ordinals: the third of May.

learn.title: Fractions & decimals
learn.html:
½a half / one and a half
¼ · ¾a quarter · three quarters
1.5one point five
25%twenty-five per cent

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
When's your appointment?
On the twenty-first of June, at half past two.
And how much was it?
Fifty dollars — with a ten per cent discount.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the two floor  ✓ the second floor. (order → ordinal)
✗
five and half  ✓ five and a half.
✗
the fiveth  ✓ the fifth.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Cardinal = counting; ordinal = order (first, second…).
  • Most ordinals add -th; watch first, second, third, fifth, ninth, twelfth.
  • Dates and floors use ordinals: the third of May, the fifth floor.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: В английском есть количественные числа для счёта (one, two, three) и порядковые для порядка (first, second, third): I have two sisters. · her second birthday.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Ағылшында санау үшін есептік сандар (one, two, three) және рет үшін реттік сандар (first, second, third) бар: I have two sisters. · her second birthday.

ru.title: Порядковые числа
ru.html: 1st first, 2nd second, 3rd third, 4th fourth, 5th fifth, 9th ninth, 12th twelfth. Большинство добавляют -th; 20th = twentieth. Даты порядковыми: the third of May.

kk.title: Реттік сандар
kk.html: 1st first, 2nd second, 3rd third, 4th fourth, 5th fifth, 9th ninth, 12th twelfth. Көбі -th қосады; 20th = twentieth. Күндер реттік санмен: the third of May.

ru.title: Дроби и десятичные
ru.html: ½ a half, ¼ a quarter, ¾ three quarters, 1.5 one point five, 25% twenty-five per cent.

kk.title: Бөлшектер мен ондықтар
kk.html: ½ a half, ¼ a quarter, ¾ three quarters, 1.5 one point five, 25% twenty-five per cent.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «When's your appointment?» — «On the twenty-first of June, at half past two.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «When's your appointment?» — «On the twenty-first of June, at half past two.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the two floor → the second floor. five and half → five and a half. the fiveth → the fifth.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the two floor → the second floor. five and half → five and a half. the fiveth → the fifth.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Количественные = счёт; порядковые = порядок (first, second…). Большинство +-th; особые: first, second, third, fifth, ninth, twelfth. Даты и этажи — порядковыми.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Есептік = санау; реттік = рет (first, second…). Көбі -th қосады; ерекшелер: first, second, third, fifth, ninth, twelfth. Күндер мен қабаттар реттік санмен.

activities.prompt: Match each figure to its word.
activities.why: ½ half, ¼ quarter, 1.5 one point five, 25% twenty-five per cent.

why: Correct for a decimal number, but for time...
Correct — with time we usually say one and a half hours.

options.t: The third floor, on the first of March.
The three floor, on the one of March.
options.why: ordinals: “the third floor, on the first of March.”

activities.why: Cardinals count; ordinals give order (dates, floors, rankings).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Prepositions of place say where something is; prepositions of movement say where it goes.

The keys are on the table. (place) · She went into the room. (movement)

learn.title: in / on / at (place)
learn.html:
in = inside / enclosedin the box, in a car, in London
on = on a surfaceon the wall, on the table, on a bus
at = at a pointat the door, at the station, at home

learn.title: to / into / onto (movement)
learn.html:
todirection: go to school, to the shop
intoenter: walk into the room
onto / off / out ofget onto the bus, out of the car

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Where are you?
At the station. I'm getting on the train now.
OK — I'll meet you at the exit.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'm in the bus stop.  ✓ I'm at the bus stop. (a point)
✗
go to into the room  ✓ go into the room.
✗
She's in home.  ✓ She's at home.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• in = inside; on = surface; at = a point.
  • Movement: to (direction), into (enter), onto/out of.
  • Fixed: at home, at school, at work.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Предлоги места говорят, где предмет; предлоги движения — куда он движется: The keys are on the table. · She went into the room.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Орын предлогтары зат қайда екенін, қозғалыс предлогтары қайда бара жатқанын білдіреді: The keys are on the table. · She went into the room.

ru.title: in / on / at (место)
ru.html: in = внутри (in the box, in London). on = на поверхности (on the table, on a bus). at = в точке (at the door, at home).

kk.title: in / on / at (орын)
kk.html: in = ішінде (in the box, in London). on = бетінде (on the table, on a bus). at = нүктеде (at the door, at home).

ru.title: to / into / onto (движение)
ru.html: to = направление (go to school). into = внутрь (walk into the room). onto/out of (get onto the bus, out of the car).

kk.title: to / into / onto (қозғалыс)
kk.html: to = бағыт (go to school). into = ішіне кіру (walk into the room). onto/out of (get onto the bus, out of the car).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Where are you?» — «At the station. I'm getting on the train now.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Where are you?» — «At the station. I'm getting on the train now.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: in the bus stop → at the bus stop. to into the room → into the room. in home → at home.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: in the bus stop → at the bus stop. to into the room → into the room. in home → at home.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: in = внутри; on = на поверхности; at = в точке. Движение: to (направление), into (внутрь), onto/out of. Устойчивые: at home, at school, at work.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: in = ішінде; on = бетінде; at = нүктеде. Қозғалыс: to (бағыт), into (кіру), onto/out of. Тұрақты: at home, at school, at work.

activities.why: a point → at; 'at home' is fixed.
in a car/box; on a bus/the floor; at the station/door.

activities.q: “I go ___ work by bus, and I stay ___ work until five.”

activities.scene: Tell a friend where you are.

options.t: At the café, on the corner of Main Street.
In the café at the corner in Main Street.
options.why: “At the café, on the corner of Main Street.”

activities.why: in = inside, on = surface, at = point; movement uses to/into/onto.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
These words say exactly where something is in relation to another thing.

The bank is next to the shop. · The car is in front of the house.

learn.title: Common ones
learn.html:
next to / besideright at the side of
betweenwith something on each side
oppositefacing, on the other side
behind / in front ofat the back / at the front
near / far fromclose / not close

learn.title: inside / outside / above / below
learn.html:
inside ↔ outside · above ↔ below · under = directly below · among = surrounded by many.

The cat is under the table. · a house among the trees.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Where's the pharmacy?
It's opposite the bank, next to the bakery.
Is it near the station?
Yes, just behind it.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
next the bank  ✓ next to the bank.
✗
in front the house  ✓ in front of the house.
✗
between the two shop  ✓ between the two shops.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• next to / beside, between, opposite, behind, in front of.
  • Don't forget the small words: next to, in front of.
  • between = two sides; among = many around.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Эти слова говорят, где именно предмет относительно другого: The bank is next to the shop. · The car is in front of the house.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бұл сөздер зат басқа затқа қатысты дәл қайда екенін көрсетеді: The bank is next to the shop. · The car is in front of the house.

ru.title: Частые
ru.html: next to/beside (рядом), between (между), opposite (напротив), behind/in front of (сзади/спереди), near/far from (близко/далеко).

kk.title: Жиі кездесетіндер
kk.html: next to/beside (жанында), between (арасында), opposite (қарсысында), behind/in front of (артында/алдында), near/far from (жақын/алыс).

ru.title: inside / outside / above / below
ru.html: inside ↔ outside; above ↔ below; under = прямо под; among = среди многих: The cat is under the table.

kk.title: inside / outside / above / below
kk.html: inside ↔ outside; above ↔ below; under = тікелей астында; among = көптің арасында: The cat is under the table.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Where's the pharmacy?» — «It's opposite the bank, next to the bakery.» — «Is it near the station?» — «Just behind it.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Where's the pharmacy?» — «It's opposite the bank, next to the bakery.» — «Is it near the station?» — «Just behind it.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: next the bank → next to the bank. in front the house → in front of the house. the two shop → the two shops.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: next the bank → next to the bank. in front the house → in front of the house. the two shop → the two shops.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: next to/beside, between, opposite, behind, in front of. Не забывайте маленькие слова: next to, in front of. between = две стороны; among = много вокруг.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: next to/beside, between, opposite, behind, in front of. Кіші сөздерді ұмытпаңыз: next to, in front of. between = екі жақ; among = көп.

activities.q: “The lamp is ___ the sofa and the chair.”
why: Correct — one thing on each side → between.

activities.why: next to; in front of — keep the small words.

activities.prompt: Match each word to its opposite.
activities.why: in front of↔behind, inside↔outside, above↔below, near↔far from.

activities.before: You can't see the garden — it's

options.t: Against the wall, between the two windows.
options.why: between = one window on each side.

options.t: Against the wall, among the two windows.

activities.why: Use next to/opposite/between/behind/in front of, keeping the small words (to, of).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Use a/an when we mention a countable thing for the first time or it's one of many. Use the when it's clear which one we mean.

I saw a dog. The dog was huge.

learn.title: a or an?
learn.html:
a + consonant sounda book, a university (/ju:/)
an + vowel soundan apple, an hour (silent h)
⭐
It's about the sound, not the letter: a university, an hour.

learn.title: When to use 'the'
learn.html:
second mentiona cat → the cat
only onethe sun, the moon
clear from contextClose the door. (this room's door)
superlativesthe best, the tallest

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
I bought a jacket and a hat.
Nice! Can I see the jacket?
Sure. It was the last one in the shop.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a hour  ✓ an hour. (vowel sound)
✗
Close a door.  ✓ Close the door. (we know which)
✗
an university  ✓ a university.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• a/an = one, first mention, one of many.
  • the = we know exactly which one.
  • a/an by sound: a book, an egg, an hour, a university.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: a/an — когда упоминаем исчисляемое впервые или это один из многих. the — когда ясно, какой именно: I saw a dog. The dog was huge.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: a/an — саналатын затты алғаш атағанда немесе көптің бірі болғанда. the — қайсысы екені түсінікті болғанда: I saw a dog. The dog was huge.

ru.title: a или an?
ru.html: a + согласный звук (a book, a university /ju:/). an + гласный звук (an apple, an hour — немая h). Важен звук, а не буква.

kk.title: a әлде an?
kk.html: a + дауыссыз дыбыс (a book, a university /ju:/). an + дауысты дыбыс (an apple, an hour — h үнсіз). Мәні — дыбыс, әріп емес.

ru.title: Когда 'the'
ru.html: При повторном упоминании (a cat → the cat), если единственный (the sun), ясно из контекста (Close the door), превосходная степень (the best).

kk.title: 'the'-ны қашан
kk.html: Екінші рет атағанда (a cat → the cat), жалғыз болса (the sun), контекстен түсінікті (Close the door), superlative (the best).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «I bought a jacket and a hat.» — «Can I see the jacket?» — «It was the last one in the shop.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «I bought a jacket and a hat.» — «Can I see the jacket?» — «It was the last one in the shop.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a hour → an hour. Close a door → Close the door. an university → a university.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a hour → an hour. Close a door → Close the door. an university → a university.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: a/an = один, первое упоминание, один из многих. the = известно, какой именно. a/an по звуку: a book, an egg, an hour, a university.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: a/an = бір, алғашқы атау, көптің бірі. the = қайсысы екені белгілі. a/an дыбыс бойынша: a book, an egg, an hour, a university.

why: 'hour' starts with a vowel sound (h is silent).

activities.why: By sound: an apple, a university (/ju:/).
first mention, one of many → a museum.

activities.before: I read a book and a magazine.

activities.q: “Can you turn off ___ light? It's too bright.”
why: Correct — we know which one → the light.

activities.why: 'engineer' starts with a vowel sound → an engineer.

options.t: I found a wallet. The wallet had 100 dollars in it.
I found the wallet. A wallet had 100 dollars.

activities.why: a/an for first mention/one of many; the when it's clear which one.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Sometimes we use no article at all (the "zero article") — especially with general plurals and uncountables.

I like music. · Dogs are friendly. (dogs in general)

learn.title: No article with...
learn.html:
general pluralsI love animals.
uncountables (general)She studies history.
meals, games, languageshave lunch, play tennis, speak French
most places/people namesTurkey, Anna, Monday

learn.title: the vs no article
learn.html:
general → no articlespecific → the
I like coffee.The coffee here is great.
Life is short.The life of a doctor is hard.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Do you like sport?
Yes! I play football and I love swimming.
Do you have breakfast before training?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I like the music. (in general)  ✓ I like music.
✗
She plays the tennis.  ✓ She plays tennis.
✗
I have the lunch at 1.  ✓ I have lunch at 1.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• No article for general plurals & uncountables.
  • No article with meals, games, languages, names.
  • Use the when you mean a specific one.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Иногда артикль не ставим («нулевой артикль») — особенно с общими множественными и неисчисляемыми: I like music. · Dogs are friendly.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейде артикль қоймаймыз («нөлдік артикль») — әсіресе жалпы көпше мен саналмайтындарда: I like music. · Dogs are friendly.

ru.title: Без артикля...
ru.html: Общие множ. (I love animals), неисчисл. (studies history), еда/игры/языки (have lunch, play tennis, speak French), имена (Turkey, Monday).

kk.title: Артикльсіз...
kk.html: Жалпы көпше (I love animals), саналмайтын (studies history), тамақ/ойын/тіл (have lunch, play tennis, speak French), атаулар (Turkey, Monday).

ru.title: the или без артикля
ru.html: Вообще → без артикля (I like coffee). Конкретно → the (The coffee here is great).

kk.title: the әлде артикльсіз
kk.html: Жалпы → артикльсіз (I like coffee). Нақты → the (The coffee here is great).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Do you like sport?» — «Yes! I play football and I love swimming.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Do you like sport?» — «Yes! I play football and I love swimming.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I like the music (вообще) → I like music. plays the tennis → plays tennis. have the lunch → have lunch.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I like the music (жалпы) → I like music. plays the tennis → plays tennis. have the lunch → have lunch.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Общие множ. и неисчисл. — без артикля. Еда, игры, языки, имена — тоже. Конкретное — the.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Жалпы көпше мен саналмайтындарға артикль жоқ. Тамақ, ойын, тіл, атауларға да. Нақты бірді айтсаңыз — the.

items.s: We have the lunch at one.

activities.why: Languages and meals take no article.

activities.after: coffee in this café is excellent. (specific)

activities.prompt: Which take 'the' and which take no article (general use)?
activities.why: the sun/moon/best; no article for music/French/tennis.

activities.q: “___ is important for your body.” (water in general)

activities.why: a school subject in general → no article: “study history.”

options.t: I like jazz, but I love the music from this film.
options.why: general 'jazz' (no article) vs specific 'the music from this film'.

options.t: I like the jazz, but I love music from this film.
options.why: General → 'jazz'; specific → 'the music from this film'.

activities.why: No article for general plurals/uncountables, meals, games, languages; the for specific things.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Pronouns replace nouns so we don't repeat them. They change with their job in the sentence.

I like her. She likes me. This book is mine.

learn.title: The main sets
learn.html:
subjectI, you, he, she, it, we, they
objectme, you, him, her, it, us, them
possessivemine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs

Subject does the action; object receives it: He called her.

learn.title: this / that / one
learn.html:
this / these = near; that / those = far. one/ones replaces a countable noun already mentioned.

I like the red one, not the blue ones. · This is my seat.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Is this bag yours?
No, mine is the black one. That's hers.
OK, I'll give it to her.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Me like it.  ✓ I like it. (subject)
✗
It's mines.  ✓ It's mine.
✗
Give it to she.  ✓ Give it to her. (object)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Subject (I) does; object (me) receives.
  • Possessive pronouns: mine, yours, hers… (no apostrophe, no noun after).
  • this/that point; one/ones replace a countable noun.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Местоимения заменяют существительные. Меняются по своей роли в предложении: I like her. She likes me. This book is mine.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Есімдіктер зат есімді қайталамау үшін алмастырады. Сөйлемдегі қызметіне қарай өзгереді: I like her. She likes me. This book is mine.

ru.title: Основные группы
ru.html: Подлежащее: I, you, he, she, it, we, they. Дополнение: me, you, him, her, it, us, them. Притяжательные: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.

kk.title: Негізгі топтар
kk.html: Бастауыш: I, you, he, she, it, we, they. Толықтауыш: me, you, him, her, it, us, them. Тәуелдік: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.

ru.title: this / that / one
ru.html: this/these = близко; that/those = далеко. one/ones заменяет уже названное исчисляемое: the red one.

kk.title: this / that / one
kk.html: this/these = жақын; that/those = алыс. one/ones — бұрын аталған саналатын зат есімді алмастырады: the red one.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Is this bag yours?» — «No, mine is the black one. That's hers.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Is this bag yours?» — «No, mine is the black one. That's hers.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Me like it → I like it. It's mines → It's mine. Give it to she → Give it to her.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Me like it → I like it. It's mines → It's mine. Give it to she → Give it to her.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Подлежащее (I) действует; дополнение (me) принимает. Притяжательные: mine, yours, hers (без апострофа, без сущ. после). this/that указывают; one/ones заменяют.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Бастауыш (I) істейді; толықтауыш (me) қабылдайды. Тәуелдік: mine, yours, hers (апострофсыз, соңында зат есім жоқ). this/that көрсетеді; one/ones алмастырады.

why: 'I' is a subject, not an object.

activities.before: I saw Anna and gave the keys to

activities.prompt: Match each subject pronoun to its possessive.

activities.before: I don't want these shoes. I like the black

options.t: I like the red one. Is it yours?
options.why: one replaces scarf; yours/hers are possessives.

options.t: I like the red. Is it your?

activities.why: Subject vs object pronouns, possessives (mine/hers), and one/ones to avoid repeating a noun.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Indefinite pronouns talk about people, things or places without saying exactly which. They combine some/any/no/every + -body/-one/-thing/-where.

Somebody is at the door. · I need something to eat.

learn.title: The pattern
learn.html:
some-somebody, something, somewhere (positive)
any-anybody, anything, anywhere (− / ?)
no-nobody, nothing, nowhere (= not any)
every-everybody, everything, everywhere (all)

learn.title: some / any / no
learn.html:
positivenegative / question
I saw somebody.I didn't see anybody.
—Is there anything to eat?
⭐
nobody = not anybody. Don't use two negatives: I didn't see nobody → I saw nobody / didn't see anybody. These pronouns take a singular verb: Everybody is here.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Is anybody home?
No, there's nobody here. Everybody went out.
Did they leave anything for dinner?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I don't know nothing.  ✓ I don't know anything. / I know nothing.
✗
Everybody are happy.  ✓ Everybody is happy. (singular)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• some- (positive), any- (neg/questions), no- (= not any), every- (all).
  • Never use two negatives together.
  • They take a singular verb: everyone is…

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Неопределённые местоимения говорят о человеке/предмете/месте, не уточняя. some/any/no/every + -body/-one/-thing/-where: Somebody is at the door.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Белгісіздік есімдіктері қайсысы екенін нақтыламай адам/зат/орын туралы айтады. some/any/no/every + -body/-one/-thing/-where: Somebody is at the door.

ru.title: Модель
ru.html: some- (утвержд.): somebody, something. any- (−/?): anybody, anything. no- (= not any): nobody, nothing. every- (все): everybody, everything.

kk.title: Үлгісі
kk.html: some- (болымды): somebody, something. any- (−/?): anybody, anything. no- (= not any): nobody, nothing. every- (барлығы): everybody, everything.

ru.title: some / any / no
ru.html: Утвержд.: I saw somebody. Отриц.: I didn't see anybody. nobody = not anybody. Двойного отрицания нет. Глагол в единственном: Everybody is here.

kk.title: some / any / no
kk.html: Болымды: I saw somebody. Болымсыз: I didn't see anybody. nobody = not anybody. Қос болымсыз болмайды. Етістік жекеше: Everybody is here.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Is anybody home?» — «No, there's nobody here. Everybody went out.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Is anybody home?» — «No, there's nobody here. Everybody went out.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I don't know nothing → I don't know anything / I know nothing. Everybody are happy → Everybody is happy.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I don't know nothing → I don't know anything / I know nothing. Everybody are happy → Everybody is happy.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: some- (утвержд.), any- (−/?), no- (= not any), every- (все). Без двойного отрицания. Глагол в ед. числе: everyone is…

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: some- (болымды), any- (−/?), no- (= not any), every- (барлығы). Қос болымсыз қолданбаңыз. Етістік жекеше: everyone is…

activities.why: No double negative; singular verb after everybody.

activities.before: The house was empty. There was

activities.after: but I can't find my glasses. (in every place)

activities.q: “Is there ___ I can do to help?”
why: In a neutral question we use any-.

options.t: Yes, but I can't find it anywhere.
Yes, but I can't find it nowhere.
No, nobody saw anything.
No, nobody didn't see nothing.

activities.why: some- (positive), any- (neg/questions), no- (= not any), every- (all) + singular verb; avoid double negatives.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
We show who owns something with possessive adjectives (before a noun) and possessive pronouns (alone).

This is my bag. · This bag is mine.

learn.title: The two sets
learn.html:
adjective (+ noun)my, your, his, her, its, our, their
pronoun (alone)mine, yours, his, hers, —, ours, theirs

It's her car. → The car is hers.

learn.title: his / her / its
learn.html:
Choose by the owner, not the object: Tom → his; Anna → her; a dog/thing → its.

Tom loves his job. · Anna called her mum. · The cat licked its paw.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Is this your phone?
No, mine's in my bag. Maybe it's his.
Or hers — let's ask.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It's her's.  ✓ It's hers. (no apostrophe)
✗
This is mine car.  ✓ This is my car. / This car is mine.
✗
Anna forgot his bag. (Anna)  ✓ Anna forgot her bag.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Adjective + noun: my, your, his, her, our, their.
  • Pronoun alone: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.
  • Match the owner (his/her/its); no apostrophe on possessive pronouns.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Принадлежность показываем притяжательными прилагательными (перед сущ.) и притяжательными местоимениями (отдельно): This is my bag. · This bag is mine.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Иелікті тәуелдік сын есімдер (зат есімнің алдында) және тәуелдік есімдіктер (жеке) арқылы көрсетеміз: This is my bag. · This bag is mine.

ru.title: Две группы
ru.html: Прилагательное (+ сущ.): my, your, his, her, its, our, their. Местоимение (отдельно): mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.

kk.title: Екі топ
kk.html: Сын есім (+ зат есім): my, your, his, her, its, our, their. Есімдік (жеке): mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.

ru.title: his / her / its
ru.html: Выбирайте по владельцу: Tom → his; Anna → her; животное/предмет → its.

kk.title: his / her / its
kk.html: Иесіне қарай таңдаңыз: Tom → his; Anna → her; жануар/зат → its.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Is this your phone?» — «No, mine's in my bag. Maybe it's his.» — «Or hers.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Is this your phone?» — «No, mine's in my bag. Maybe it's his.» — «Or hers.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: her's → hers. mine car → my car / car is mine. Anna → her bag.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: her's → hers. mine car → my car / car is mine. Anna → her bag.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Прилагательное + сущ.: my, your, his… Отдельное местоимение: mine, yours, hers… По владельцу (his/her/its); без апострофа.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Сын есім + зат есім: my, your, his… Жеке есімдік: mine, yours, hers… Иесіне сәйкес (his/her/its); тәуелдік есімдіктерде апостроф жоқ.

activities.why: No apostrophe on hers; 'my' before a noun.

activities.prompt: Match each possessive adjective to its pronoun.

activities.q: “Your bag is black; ___ is red.” (my bag)

options.t: No, mine is blue. That one's hers.
No, my is blue. That one's her.

activities.why: Possessive adjectives go before nouns (my); possessive pronouns stand alone (mine).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
For people and animals, show possession with 's. For things, we often use of.

Jane's house · the roof of the house

learn.title: Where does 's go?
learn.html:
singularthe boy's bike, my sister's car
plural ending in -sthe boys' bikes (apostrophe only)
irregular pluralthe children's toys, the men's room

learn.title: 's vs of
learn.html:
's (people/animals)of (things)
Anna's phonethe end of the film
the dog's tailthe top of the page

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Whose car is that?
It's my brother's. He parks at the end of the street.
And the keys? Are they the neighbours'?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the house of Jane  ✓ Jane's house.
✗
the childrens' toys  ✓ the children's toys.
✗
the leg of the table's  ✓ the leg of the table.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• People/animals → 's (or s' for regular plurals).
  • Things → the … of the ….
  • Irregular plural → 's: children's, men's, women's.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Для людей и животных — 's; для вещей — часто of: Jane's house · the roof of the house.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Адам мен жануарларға иелікті 's арқылы; заттарға жиі of арқылы: Jane's house · the roof of the house.

ru.title: Куда ставить 's?
ru.html: Ед. число: the boy's bike. Множ. на -s: the boys' bikes (только апостроф). Неправильное множ.: the children's toys.

kk.title: 's қайда қойылады?
kk.html: Жекеше: the boy's bike. -s-пен біткен көпше: the boys' bikes (тек апостроф). Бұрыс көпше: the children's toys.

ru.title: 's и of
ru.html: 's — люди/животные (Anna's phone, the dog's tail). of — вещи (the end of the film, the top of the page).

kk.title: 's пен of
kk.html: 's — адам/жануар (Anna's phone, the dog's tail). of — зат (the end of the film, the top of the page).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Whose car is that?» — «It's my brother's. He parks at the end of the street.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Whose car is that?» — «It's my brother's. He parks at the end of the street.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the house of Jane → Jane's house. the childrens' toys → the children's toys. the leg of the table's → the leg of the table.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the house of Jane → Jane's house. the childrens' toys → the children's toys. the leg of the table's → the leg of the table.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Люди/животные → 's (или s' для правильн. множ.). Вещи → the … of the …. Неправильное множ. → 's: children's, men's.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Адам/жануар → 's (немесе дұрыс көпшеде s'). Зат → the … of the …. Бұрыс көпше → 's: children's, men's.

why: Correct — a thing → the colour of the sky.

activities.after: toys are on the floor. (the boys)

steps.npc: Whose jacket is on the chair?

options.t: It's my sister's. Put it near the end of the sofa.
It's the jacket of my sister. Put it near the sofa's end.

activities.why: 's for people/animals, of for things; irregular plurals take 's, regular plurals take s'.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Adjectives describe nouns (people, things, places). In English they never change for plural and usually come before the noun.

a tall man · beautiful flowers

learn.title: Where they go
learn.html:
before a nouna red car, an interesting book
after be / seem / lookThe car is red. · You look tired.
⭐
Adjectives don't add -s: reds cars → red cars.

learn.title: Making them stronger/weaker
learn.html:
Use very, really, quite, a bit before an adjective.

It's very cold. · I'm a bit tired. · That's really good.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
How was the film?
Really good! The story was exciting and the actors were brilliant.
The cinema was a bit cold, though.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a car red  ✓ a red car. (before the noun)
✗
They are happys.  ✓ They are happy. (no plural)
✗
She is a nice.  ✓ She is nice. / She is a nice person.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Adjectives describe nouns; they never take -s.
  • Before a noun, or after be/look/seem.
  • Strengthen with very/really; soften with quite/a bit.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Прилагательные описывают существительные. В английском они не меняются во множественном и обычно стоят перед существительным: a tall man · beautiful flowers.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Сын есімдер зат есімді сипаттайды. Ағылшында олар көпше үшін өзгермейді және әдетте зат есімнің алдында тұрады: a tall man · beautiful flowers.

ru.title: Где стоят
ru.html: Перед сущ. (a red car) или после be/seem/look (The car is red. You look tired). Без -s.

kk.title: Қайда тұрады
kk.html: Зат есімнің алдында (a red car), немесе be/seem/look-тан кейін (The car is red. You look tired). -s қоспайды.

ru.title: Усиление/смягчение
ru.html: Перед прилагательным very, really, quite, a bit: It's very cold. I'm a bit tired.

kk.title: Күшейту/әлсірету
kk.html: Сын есімнің алдында very, really, quite, a bit: It's very cold. I'm a bit tired.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «How was the film?» — «Really good! The story was exciting and the actors were brilliant.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «How was the film?» — «Really good! The story was exciting and the actors were brilliant.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a car red → a red car. They are happys → They are happy. She is a nice → She is nice.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a car red → a red car. They are happys → They are happy. She is a nice → She is nice.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Прилагательные описывают сущ.; без -s. Перед сущ. или после be/look/seem. very/really усиливают; quite/a bit смягчают.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Сын есімдер зат есімді сипаттайды; -s алмайды. Зат есімнің алдында немесе be/look/seem-нен кейін. very/really күшейтеді; quite/a bit жұмсартады.

activities.why: No plural -s; adjective before the noun.

activities.q: “The room is ___ small, but it's fine.”

activities.scene: Tell a friend about a hotel.

options.t: It was a lovely, quiet hotel near the sea.
It was a hotel lovely and quiets.

activities.why: Adjectives go before nouns (or after be/look/seem) and never take a plural -s.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
When two or more adjectives describe one noun, they follow a usual order. And some adjectives end in -ed or -ing with different meanings.

a big red car · I'm bored / it's boring

learn.title: Usual adjective order
learn.html:
opinion → size → age → colour → origin → material → noun

a lovely little old houseopinion-size-age
a big red Italian carsize-colour-origin

learn.title: -ed vs -ing adjectives
learn.html:
-ed = how you feel-ing = what causes the feeling
I'm bored.The lesson is boring.
She's interested.The book is interesting.
We were tired.The trip was tiring.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
How was the museum?
Amazing! But I was exhausted after — it was a long tiring day.
Were the guides interesting?
Yes, really interested in their subject!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
a red big car  ✓ a big red car. (size before colour)
✗
I'm boring. (= I make people bored!)  ✓ I'm bored. (feeling)
✗
The film was interested.  ✓ The film was interesting.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Order: opinion, size, age, colour, origin, material + noun.
  • -ed = how a person feels (bored, tired).
  • -ing = the thing causing the feeling (boring, tiring).

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Когда несколько прилагательных описывают одно сущ., есть обычный порядок. А некоторые прилагательные на -ed/-ing различаются по смыслу: a big red car · I'm bored / it's boring.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Бір зат есімді бірнеше сын есім сипаттағанда белгілі рет болады. Кейбір сын есімдер -ed не -ing-пен бітеді, мағынасы бөлек: a big red car · I'm bored / it's boring.

ru.title: Обычный порядок
ru.html: мнение → размер → возраст → цвет → происхождение → материал → сущ.: a lovely little old house; a big red Italian car.

kk.title: Әдеттегі рет
kk.html: пікір → өлшем → жас → түс → шыққан жері → материал → зат есім: a lovely little old house; a big red Italian car.

ru.title: -ed и -ing
ru.html: -ed = чувство (I'm bored). -ing = причина чувства (The lesson is boring). tired/tiring, interested/interesting.

kk.title: -ed пен -ing
kk.html: -ed = сезім (I'm bored). -ing = сезімнің себебі (The lesson is boring). tired/tiring, interested/interesting.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «How was the museum?» — «Amazing! But I was exhausted — it was a long tiring day.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «How was the museum?» — «Amazing! But I was exhausted — it was a long tiring day.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: a red big car → a big red car. I'm boring → I'm bored. The film was interested → interesting.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: a red big car → a big red car. I'm boring → I'm bored. The film was interested → interesting.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Порядок: мнение, размер, возраст, цвет, происхождение, материал + сущ. -ed = чувство человека; -ing = то, что вызывает чувство.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Рет: пікір, өлшем, жас, түс, шыққан жері, материал + зат есім. -ed = адамның сезімі; -ing = сезім тудыратын нәрсе.

activities.q: “The lesson was so ___. I nearly fell asleep.”

activities.prompt: I was very boring at the party.
activities.answer: i was very bored at the party
alts: i was very bored at the party.

options.t: Amazing — but the queues were tiring and I got bored waiting.
Amazing — but the queues were tired and I got boring.

activities.why: Follow the adjective order; -ed = the feeling, -ing = what causes it.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Comparatives compare two things. Short adjectives add -er; long adjectives use more. We join them with than.

Lisa is older than Clara. · This is more expensive than that.

learn.title: How to form them
learn.html:
1 syllable + erold → older, cheap → cheaper
-e → -ernice → nicer
1 vowel+consonant → doublebig → bigger, hot → hotter
-y → -ierhappy → happier, easy → easier
2+ syllables → moremore careful, more expensive

learn.title: Irregular & than
learn.html: Irregular: good → better, bad → worse, far → further. Use than to compare: much/a bit can go before: much bigger, a bit colder.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Is the bus faster than the train?
No, the train is quicker and more comfortable.
But the bus is cheaper, isn't it?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
more big  ✓ bigger.
✗
more better  ✓ better.
✗
older that me  ✓ older than me.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Short adjective + -er; long adjective → more.
  • Compare with than.
  • Irregular: better, worse, further; never 'more better'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Сравнительная степень сравнивает два предмета. Короткие + -er; длинные → more. Соединяем через than: Lisa is older than Clara.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Салыстырмалы шырай екі затты салыстырады. Қысқа сын есім -er қосады; ұзыны more алады. than арқылы жалғаймыз: Lisa is older than Clara.

ru.title: Как образуются
ru.html: 1 слог + er (older). -e → -er (nicer). гласный+согласный → удвоение (bigger). -y → -ier (happier). 2+ слога → more (more expensive).

kk.title: Қалай жасалады
kk.html: 1 буын + er (older). -e → -er (nicer). дауысты+дауыссыз → екілену (bigger). -y → -ier (happier). 2+ буын → more (more expensive).

ru.title: Неправильные & than
ru.html: good → better, bad → worse, far → further. Сравнение: than. Перед ними much/a bit: much bigger, a bit colder.

kk.title: Бұрыс тұлғалар & than
kk.html: good → better, bad → worse, far → further. Салыстыру: than. Алдында much/a bit: much bigger, a bit colder.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Is the bus faster than the train?» — «No, the train is quicker and more comfortable.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Is the bus faster than the train?» — «No, the train is quicker and more comfortable.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: more big → bigger. more better → better. older that me → older than me.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: more big → bigger. more better → better. older that me → older than me.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Короткое + -er; длинное → more. Через than. Неправильные: better, worse, further; 'more better' нельзя.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Қысқа сын есім + -er; ұзын → more. than арқылы. Бұрыс: better, worse, further; 'more better' болмайды.

activities.q: “My cold is ___ today — I feel awful.”

options.t: The second one — it's bigger and more modern than the first.
options.why: short → bigger; long → more modern; than.

options.t: The second one — it's more big and modern than the first.

activities.why: Short adjectives add -er, long ones take more, and comparisons use than.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Superlatives compare three or more and pick the top. Short adjectives add -est; long ones use the most. Always with the.

the tallest building · the most interesting book

learn.title: How to form them
learn.html:
1 syllable → the -estthe fastest, the biggest
-y → -iestthe happiest, the easiest
2+ syllables → the mostthe most careful, the most famous
irregularthe best, the worst, the furthest

learn.title: the + superlative + in/of
learn.html: Use in for places/groups, of for a set: the tallest in the class, the best of all. Often with the present perfect + ever: the best film I've ever seen.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
What's the highest mountain in the world?
Everest. And Japan was the most amazing place I've ever visited.
Best trip ever?
Definitely!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
the most tallest  ✓ the tallest.
✗
the most good  ✓ the best.
✗
biggest city (no the)  ✓ the biggest city.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Short adjective → the -est; long → the most.
  • Always use the; irregular: the best/worst.
  • For 3+ things; use in the world/class, best … ever.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Превосходная степень сравнивает три и более и выбирает наивысшее. Короткие + -est; длинные → the most. Всегда с the: the tallest building.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Күшейтпелі шырай үш не одан көп заттан ең жоғарысын таңдайды. Қысқа сын есім -est қосады; ұзыны the most. Әрқашан the-мен: the tallest building.

ru.title: Как образуются
ru.html: 1 слог → the -est (the fastest, the biggest). -y → -iest (the happiest). 2+ слога → the most (the most famous). Неправильные: the best/worst/furthest.

kk.title: Қалай жасалады
kk.html: 1 буын → the -est (the fastest, the biggest). -y → -iest (the happiest). 2+ буын → the most (the most famous). Бұрыс: the best/worst/furthest.

ru.title: the + superlative + in/of
ru.html: Для мест/групп in, для набора of: the tallest in the class, the best of all. Часто present perfect + ever: the best film I've ever seen.

kk.title: the + superlative + in/of
kk.html: Орын/топ үшін in, жиын үшін of: the tallest in the class, the best of all. Жиі present perfect + ever: the best film I've ever seen.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «What's the highest mountain in the world?» — «Everest. Japan was the most amazing place I've ever visited.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «What's the highest mountain in the world?» — «Everest. Japan was the most amazing place I've ever visited.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: the most tallest → the tallest. the most good → the best. biggest city (без the) → the biggest city.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: the most tallest → the tallest. the most good → the best. biggest city (the жоқ) → the biggest city.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Короткое → the -est; длинное → the most. Всегда the; неправильные: the best/worst. Для 3+; in the world/class, best … ever.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Қысқа → the -est; ұзын → the most. Әрқашан the; бұрыс: the best/worst. 3+ зат үшін; in the world/class, best … ever.

activities.q: “Everest is the ___ mountain in the world.”

activities.answer: it's the best film i've ever seen
alts: the best film i've ever seen
it's the best film i've ever seen.

steps.npc: What's the best meal you've ever had?

options.t: The most delicious meal was in Italy — the best pizza ever!
options.why: the most delicious; the best … ever.

options.t: The deliciousest meal was in Italy — the most best pizza!

activities.why: Short adjectives take the -est, long ones the most; always with the (best/worst are irregular).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Adverbs usually describe a verb — they say how something happens. Most are made by adding -ly to an adjective.

She sings beautifully. · He drove carefully.

learn.title: Making adverbs
learn.html:
adjective + lyquick → quickly, slow → slowly
-y → -ilyhappy → happily, easy → easily
irregulargood → well, fast → fast, hard → hard

learn.title: Where adverbs go
learn.html:
manner (how)usually after the verb/object: He speaks English well.
frequency (how often)before the main verb: I always walk. · She is often late.
⭐
Frequency adverbs go after be but before other verbs: She is always happy; She always smiles.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
You speak English really well!
Thanks — I practise every day. I usually watch films in English.
That works — you learn quickly.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
She sings good.  ✓ She sings well.
✗
I speak fastly.  ✓ I speak fast. (no -ly)
✗
I go always to bed late.  ✓ I always go to bed late.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Adverb of manner = adjective + -ly (well, fast, hard are irregular).
  • Manner adverbs go after the verb/object.
  • Frequency adverbs: after be, before other verbs.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Наречия обычно описывают глагол — говорят, как происходит действие. Большинство образуются прибавлением -ly: She sings beautifully.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Үстеулер әдетте етістікті сипаттайды — әрекеттің қалай болатынын айтады. Көбі сын есімге -ly қосу арқылы жасалады: She sings beautifully.

ru.title: Образование наречий
ru.html: прилаг. + ly (quickly, slowly). -y → -ily (happily). Неправильные: good → well, fast → fast, hard → hard.

kk.title: Үстеу жасау
kk.html: сын есім + ly (quickly, slowly). -y → -ily (happily). Бұрыс: good → well, fast → fast, hard → hard.

ru.title: Место наречия
ru.html: Образа действия (как): после глагола/дополнения (speaks English well). Частотности: перед основным глаголом (I always walk), но после be (She is always happy).

kk.title: Үстеу қайда тұрады
kk.html: Сын-сипат (қалай): етістік/толықтауыштан кейін (speaks English well). Жиілік: негізгі етістіктің алдында (I always walk), бірақ be-дан кейін (She is always happy).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «You speak English really well!» — «Thanks — I practise every day. I usually watch films in English.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «You speak English really well!» — «Thanks — I practise every day. I usually watch films in English.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: sings good → sings well. fastly → fast. I go always → I always go.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: sings good → sings well. fastly → fast. I go always → I always go.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Наречие образа действия = прилаг. + -ly (well, fast, hard — неправильные). Ставится после глагола. Частотности: после be, перед другими глаголами.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Сын-сипат үстеуі = сын есім + -ly (well, fast, hard — бұрыс). Сын-сипат үстеуі етістіктен кейін. Жиілік: be-дан кейін, басқа етістіктің алдында.

options.t: I usually read carefully and I often listen to podcasts.
I read usually careful and I listen often podcasts.

activities.why: Manner adverbs (usually -ly) go after the verb; frequency adverbs go before the main verb, after be.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
There is / There are says that something exists. It is used for weather, time, distance and general facts.

There's a book on the table. · It's cold today.

learn.title: there is / there are
learn.html:
singular / uncountableThere is (There's) a car / some milk.
pluralThere are two cars.
negative / questionThere isn't … · Is there …? · Are there …?

learn.title: it for weather, time, distance
learn.html:
weatherIt's raining. · It's hot.
time / dateIt's 6 o'clock. · It's Monday.
distanceIt's 5 km to the station.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Is there a café near here?
Yes, there's one on the corner. But it's closed on Sundays.
And it's raining… let's stay in.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
It is a book on the table.  ✓ There is a book on the table.
✗
There is many people.  ✓ There are many people.
✗
Is raining.  ✓ It's raining.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• There is/are = something exists (are = plural).
  • It = weather, time, date, distance.
  • Don't drop 'it': It's raining, not 'Is raining'.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: There is / There are говорит, что что-то существует. It — для погоды, времени, расстояния, общих фактов: There's a book on the table. · It's cold.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: There is / There are бір нәрсенің бар екенін білдіреді. It — ауа райы, уақыт, қашықтық, жалпы фактілер үшін: There's a book on the table. · It's cold.

ru.title: there is / there are
ru.html: Ед./неисчисл.: There is a car / some milk. Множ.: There are two cars. Отриц./вопрос: There isn't… · Is/Are there…?

kk.title: there is / there are
kk.html: Жекеше/саналмайтын: There is a car / some milk. Көпше: There are two cars. Болымсыз/сұрақ: There isn't… · Is/Are there…?

ru.title: it — погода, время, расстояние
ru.html: Погода: It's raining. Время/дата: It's 6 o'clock / Monday. Расстояние: It's 5 km to the station.

kk.title: it — ауа райы, уақыт, қашықтық
kk.html: Ауа райы: It's raining. Уақыт/күн: It's 6 o'clock / Monday. Қашықтық: It's 5 km to the station.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Is there a café near here?» — «Yes, there's one on the corner. But it's closed on Sundays.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Is there a café near here?» — «Yes, there's one on the corner. But it's closed on Sundays.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: It is a book on the table → There is a book. There is many people → There are many people. Is raining → It's raining.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: It is a book on the table → There is a book. There is many people → There are many people. Is raining → It's raining.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: There is/are = существует (are = множ.). It = погода, время, дата, расстояние. Не опускайте 'it': It's raining.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: There is/are = бар (are = көпше). It = ауа райы, уақыт, күн, қашықтық. 'it'-ті түсірмеңіз: It's raining.

activities.q: “___ is a spider in the bath!”
why: 'It is' can't introduce something new like this.

activities.prompt: There is a bank near here.
activities.answer: is there a bank near here
alts: is there a bank near here?

activities.q: “How far is it? — ___ about two kilometres.”

options.t: There's a nice park and there are two cinemas. It's quiet at night.
options.why: there's/there are for existence; it's for general fact.

options.t: It's a nice park and there is two cinemas.
options.why: “There's a park and there are two cinemas.”

activities.why: There is/are for existence (are = plural); it for weather, time, date and distance.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
have and have got both show possession (they mean the same). get often means receive / become / arrive.

I have a car. = I've got a car. · I get the bus to work.

learn.title: have vs have got
learn.html:
have (with do)have got (with have)
I have a dog. · Do you have a dog?I've got a dog. · Have you got a dog?
She doesn't have …She hasn't got …
⭐
have got is more common in speech; both are correct for possession.

learn.title: meanings of get
learn.html:
receiveI got a present.
becomeIt's getting dark.
arriveWe got home late.
buy / fetchI'll get some bread.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Have you got any brothers?
Yes, I've got one. He gets the train to college.
Nice. It's getting late — I should go.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I have got not a car.  ✓ I haven't got a car.
✗
Do you have got …?  ✓ Have you got …? / Do you have …?
✗
I got hungry (now, always).  ✓ I'm getting hungry.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• have = have got (possession); got version is common in speech.
  • have uses do/does; have got uses have/has.
  • get = receive / become / arrive / buy.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: have и have got означают обладание (одно и то же). get часто = получать/становиться/добираться: I have = I've got a car. · I get the bus.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: have мен have got иелікті білдіреді (мағынасы бірдей). get жиі алу/болу/жету дегенді білдіреді: I have = I've got a car. · I get the bus.

ru.title: have и have got
ru.html: have — с do (Do you have…? She doesn't have…). have got — с have (Have you got…? She hasn't got…). В речи have got чаще.

kk.title: have пен have got
kk.html: have — do-мен (Do you have…? She doesn't have…). have got — have-пен (Have you got…? She hasn't got…). Сөйлеуде have got жиірек.

ru.title: значения get
ru.html: получать (I got a present), становиться (It's getting dark), добираться (We got home late), купить/принести (I'll get some bread).

kk.title: get мағыналары
kk.html: алу (I got a present), болу (It's getting dark), жету (We got home late), сатып алу/әкелу (I'll get some bread).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Have you got any brothers?» — «Yes, I've got one. He gets the train to college.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Have you got any brothers?» — «Yes, I've got one. He gets the train to college.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: have got not → haven't got. Do you have got…? → Have you got…? / Do you have…? I got hungry (сейчас) → I'm getting hungry.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: have got not → haven't got. Do you have got…? → Have you got…? / Do you have…? I got hungry (қазір) → I'm getting hungry.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: have = have got (обладание); вариант с got чаще в речи. have — do/does; have got — have/has. get = получать/становиться/добираться/покупать.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: have = have got (иелік); got нұсқасы сөйлеуде жиі. have — do/does; have got — have/has. get = алу/болу/жету/сатып алу.

items.s: I have got not a bike.
Do you have got a car?

activities.why: haven't got; don't mix do + have got.

activities.q: “What time did you get home?” 'get' means…

activities.why: have got → “Have you got a pen?” (or “Do you have a pen?”)

activities.after: a nice message from Sam yesterday. (past)

steps.npc: Have you got any plans tonight?

options.t: I've got a class, then I'll get some food on the way home.
I have got a class, then I get some food (arrive).
options.why: “I've got a class… I'll get some food” (buy).

activities.why: have = have got for possession (different question/negative forms); get = receive/become/arrive/buy.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
make, do and go appear in many fixed phrases you learn together. Phrasal verbs = verb + small word (up, on, off…) with a special meaning.

make a mistake · do homework · go shopping · look up a word

learn.title: make vs do
learn.html:
make (create/produce)do (activity/task)
make dinner, make a plan, make a noisedo homework, do the shopping, do a job

learn.title: go + -ing; phrasal verbs
learn.html:
go + activity: go shopping, go swimming, go running.

look upfind information: look up a word
turn on / offstart/stop a machine
get upleave your bed
⭐
With pronouns, the small word goes after: look it up (not look up it).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
What did you do at the weekend?
I did some housework and went shopping. You?
I made a cake and looked up some recipes.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
make my homework  ✓ do my homework.
✗
do a mistake  ✓ make a mistake.
✗
look up it  ✓ look it up.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• make = create; do = an activity/task.
  • go + -ing for activities (go swimming).
  • Phrasal verb + pronoun → pronoun in the middle: turn it off.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: make, do, go встречаются в устойчивых фразах. Фразовые глаголы = глагол + слово (up, on, off) с особым смыслом: make a mistake · do homework · go shopping.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: make, do, go көптеген тұрақты тіркестерде кездеседі. Phrasal verbs = етістік + кіші сөз (up, on, off) арнайы мағынамен: make a mistake · do homework · go shopping.

ru.title: make и do
ru.html: make (создавать): make dinner, make a plan, make a noise. do (действие/задача): do homework, do the shopping, do a job.

kk.title: make пен do
kk.html: make (жасау/өндіру): make dinner, make a plan, make a noise. do (әрекет/тапсырма): do homework, do the shopping, do a job.

ru.title: go + -ing; фразовые глаголы
ru.html: go + активность: go shopping/swimming. look up (найти информацию), turn on/off (вкл/выкл), get up (вставать). С местоимением слово в середине: look it up.

kk.title: go + -ing; phrasal verbs
kk.html: go + әрекет: go shopping/swimming. look up (ақпарат табу), turn on/off (қосу/өшіру), get up (тұру). Есімдікпен кіші сөз кейін: look it up.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «What did you do at the weekend?» — «I did some housework and went shopping.» — «I made a cake and looked up recipes.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «What did you do at the weekend?» — «I did some housework and went shopping.» — «I made a cake and looked up recipes.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: make my homework → do my homework. do a mistake → make a mistake. look up it → look it up.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: make my homework → do my homework. do a mistake → make a mistake. look up it → look it up.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: make = создавать; do = действие/задача. go + -ing (go swimming). Фразовый глагол + местоимение → местоимение в середине: turn it off.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: make = жасау; do = әрекет/тапсырма. go + -ing (go swimming). Phrasal verb + есімдік → есімдік ортада: turn it off.

activities.before: I don't know this word — I'll

why: Pronoun can't go after the particle.
Correct — pronoun in the middle → turn it off.

activities.why: with a noun: “Turn on the lights.”

options.t: I went running and made a big lunch for friends.
I did running and made the homework.

activities.why: make = create, do = activity, go + -ing; put pronouns inside phrasal verbs (look it up).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
After some verbs we use the infinitive (to + verb); after others the gerund (-ing). You learn these by verb.

I want to go. · I enjoy reading.

learn.title: Two groups
learn.html:
verb + to (infinitive)verb + -ing (gerund)
want, need, hope, decide, would like, learnenjoy, like, love, hate, finish, mind, suggest
I decided to leave.I finished eating.

learn.title: preposition → -ing
learn.html: After a preposition, always use -ing: good at cooking, interested in learning, before leaving, instead of waiting.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
What do you want to do tonight?
I'd like to relax. I enjoy watching films.
Good idea. I'm tired of working!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I want going.  ✓ I want to go.
✗
I enjoy to read.  ✓ I enjoy reading.
✗
good at cook  ✓ good at cooking.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• want/need/hope/decide + to + verb.
  • enjoy/finish/mind/suggest + -ing.
  • After any preposition → -ing.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: После одних глаголов — инфинитив (to + глагол), после других — герундий (-ing). Учат по глаголам: I want to go. · I enjoy reading.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір етістіктерден кейін инфинитив (to + етістік), басқаларынан кейін gerund (-ing) қолданылады. Етістікпен бірге жаттайсыз: I want to go. · I enjoy reading.

ru.title: Две группы
ru.html: + to: want, need, hope, decide, would like, learn (I decided to leave). + -ing: enjoy, like, love, hate, finish, mind, suggest (I finished eating).

kk.title: Екі топ
kk.html: + to: want, need, hope, decide, would like, learn (I decided to leave). + -ing: enjoy, like, love, hate, finish, mind, suggest (I finished eating).

ru.title: предлог → -ing
ru.html: После предлога всегда -ing: good at cooking, interested in learning, before leaving.

kk.title: предлог → -ing
kk.html: Предлогтан кейін әрқашан -ing: good at cooking, interested in learning, before leaving.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
