ru.html: «What do you want to do tonight?» — «I'd like to relax. I enjoy watching films.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «What do you want to do tonight?» — «I'd like to relax. I enjoy watching films.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I want going → I want to go. I enjoy to read → I enjoy reading. good at cook → good at cooking.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I want going → I want to go. I enjoy to read → I enjoy reading. good at cook → good at cooking.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: want/need/hope/decide + to + глагол. enjoy/finish/mind/suggest + -ing. После любого предлога → -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: want/need/hope/decide + to + етістік. enjoy/finish/mind/suggest + -ing. Кез келген предлогтан кейін → -ing.

activities.prompt: Which form does each verb take?

steps.npc: What do you hope to do next year?

options.t: I hope to travel, and I'm interested in learning Spanish.
I hope travelling, and I'm interested in to learn Spanish.

activities.why: want/hope/decide + to; enjoy/finish/mind + -ing; after any preposition use -ing.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Many adjectives go with a fixed preposition. These pairs must be learned together.

good at maths · afraid of dogs · interested in art

learn.title: Common pairs
learn.html:
atgood/bad at, brilliant at
ofafraid of, proud of, full of, scared of
ininterested in
withangry with, pleased with
aboutworried about, excited about, sorry about
forfamous for, ready for

learn.title: + noun or -ing
learn.html: After the preposition, use a noun or the -ing form: good at chess / good at playing chess; afraid of the dark / afraid of flying.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Are you good at cooking?
Not really, but I'm interested in learning. Are you afraid of anything?
Yes — I'm scared of spiders!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
good in maths  ✓ good at maths.
✗
afraid from dogs  ✓ afraid of dogs.
✗
interested on art  ✓ interested in art.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Learn adjective + preposition as a pair.
  • good at, afraid of, interested in, worried about, pleased with.
  • After the preposition → noun or -ing.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Многие прилагательные идут с фиксированным предлогом. Эти пары учат вместе: good at maths · afraid of dogs · interested in art.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Көп сын есім тұрақты предлогпен келеді. Бұл тіркестерді бірге жаттау керек: good at maths · afraid of dogs · interested in art.

ru.title: Частые пары
ru.html: at: good/bad at. of: afraid/proud/full/scared of. in: interested in. with: angry/pleased with. about: worried/excited/sorry about. for: famous/ready for.

kk.title: Жиі тіркестер
kk.html: at: good/bad at. of: afraid/proud/full/scared of. in: interested in. with: angry/pleased with. about: worried/excited/sorry about. for: famous/ready for.

ru.title: + сущ. или -ing
ru.html: После предлога сущ. или -ing: good at chess / good at playing chess; afraid of flying.

kk.title: + зат есім немесе -ing
kk.html: Предлогтан кейін зат есім не -ing: good at chess / good at playing chess; afraid of flying.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Are you good at cooking?» — «Not really, but I'm interested in learning. Are you afraid of anything?» — «I'm scared of spiders!»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Are you good at cooking?» — «Not really, but I'm interested in learning. Are you afraid of anything?» — «I'm scared of spiders!»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: good in maths → good at maths. afraid from dogs → afraid of dogs. interested on art → interested in art.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: good in maths → good at maths. afraid from dogs → afraid of dogs. interested on art → interested in art.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Учите прилагательное + предлог парой: good at, afraid of, interested in, worried about, pleased with. После предлога → сущ. или -ing.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Сын есім + предлогты жұп ретінде жаттаңыз: good at, afraid of, interested in, worried about, pleased with. Предлогтан кейін → зат есім не -ing.

activities.prompt: Match each adjective to its preposition.
activities.why: good at, afraid of, interested in, pleased with.

options.t: I'm interested in music, and I'm quite good at singing.
I'm interested on music, and I'm good in singing.

activities.why: Adjective + preposition are fixed pairs (good at, afraid of, interested in); after the preposition use a noun or -ing.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
"Functions" are the useful things we do with language — giving advice, agreeing, apologising and replying — each with its own fixed phrases.

You should rest. · I agree. · I'm sorry.

learn.title: Advice
learn.html:
You should / shouldn't …You should see a doctor.
Why don't you …?Why don't you call her?
If I were you, I'd …If I were you, I'd wait.

learn.title: Agreeing & apologising
learn.html:
agree / disagreeapologise / reply
I agree. / You're right. / I don't think so.I'm sorry (about that). — That's OK. / No problem.
So do I. / Me too.Thank you. — You're welcome.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
I'm so tired lately.
You should sleep more. Why don't you go to bed earlier?
You're right. Sorry for complaining!
No problem.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
You should to rest.  ✓ You should rest.
✗
I'm agree.  ✓ I agree. (agree is a verb)
✗
Why you don't call?  ✓ Why don't you call?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Advice: You should…, Why don't you…?, If I were you…
  • Agree: I agree / So do I. Disagree: I don't think so.
  • Apologise: I'm sorry. — Reply: That's OK / No problem.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: «Функции» — это то, что мы делаем языком: даём совет, соглашаемся, извиняемся, отвечаем — у каждого свои устойчивые фразы: You should rest. · I agree. · I'm sorry.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: «Функциялар» — тілмен істейтін пайдалы әрекеттер: кеңес беру, келісу, кешірім сұрау, жауап беру — әрқайсысының тұрақты тіркестері бар: You should rest. · I agree. · I'm sorry.

ru.title: Совет
ru.html: You should/shouldn't… · Why don't you…? · If I were you, I'd…

kk.title: Кеңес
kk.html: You should/shouldn't… · Why don't you…? · If I were you, I'd…

ru.title: Согласие и извинение
ru.html: Согласие: I agree / You're right / So do I / Me too. Несогласие: I don't think so. Извинение: I'm sorry — ответ: That's OK / No problem.

kk.title: Келісу мен кешірім
kk.html: Келісу: I agree / You're right / So do I / Me too. Келіспеу: I don't think so. Кешірім: I'm sorry — жауап: That's OK / No problem.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «I'm so tired.» — «You should sleep more. Why don't you go to bed earlier?» — «You're right. Sorry!» — «No problem.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «I'm so tired.» — «You should sleep more. Why don't you go to bed earlier?» — «You're right. Sorry!» — «No problem.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: should to rest → should rest. I'm agree → I agree. Why you don't call? → Why don't you call?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: should to rest → should rest. I'm agree → I agree. Why you don't call? → Why don't you call?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Совет: You should…, Why don't you…?, If I were you… Согласие: I agree / So do I. Извинение: I'm sorry — That's OK / No problem.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Кеңес: You should…, Why don't you…?, If I were you… Келісу: I agree / So do I. Кешірім: I'm sorry — That's OK / No problem.

activities.why: 'agree' is a verb; should + base verb.

activities.prompt: Match each phrase to what it does.

activities.why: reply to sorry → No problem / That's OK.

activities.prompt: Your friend can't sleep. (drink less coffee)
activities.answer: if i were you, i'd drink less coffee
alts: if i were you i'd drink less coffee
if i were you, i would drink less coffee
if i were you i would drink less coffee
activities.why: “If I were you, I'd drink less coffee.”

activities.scene: A friend is stressed about exams.

steps.npc: I'm so stressed about my exam.

options.t: You should take breaks. Why don't you make a study plan?
You should to take breaks. Why you don't plan?

activities.why: Use fixed phrases: should/Why don't you (advice), I agree (agreeing), I'm sorry / No problem (apology & reply).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
More everyday functions: describing people/things, giving directions, making excuses and greeting people.

What's she like? · Turn left. · How are you?

learn.title: Descriptions
learn.html:
What's he like?character: He's kind and funny.
What does she look like?appearance: She's tall with dark hair.
What's it like?It's big and modern.

learn.title: Directions & greetings
learn.html:
directionsgreetings
Go straight on. Turn left/right. It's on your left.Hello / Hi. How are you? — Fine, thanks. Nice to meet you.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Hi! How are you?
Fine, thanks. Sorry I'm late — the bus was slow.
No worries. The café's just ahead — turn right at the corner.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
How is she like?  ✓ What's she like?
✗
What she looks like?  ✓ What does she look like?
✗
Go to straight.  ✓ Go straight on.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• What's … like? (character); What does … look like? (appearance).
  • Directions: go straight, turn left/right, on your left.
  • Greetings: How are you? — Fine, thanks. Nice to meet you.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Ещё повседневные функции: описание людей/вещей, указания дороги, оправдания и приветствия: What's she like? · Turn left. · How are you?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Тағы да күнделікті функциялар: адам/затты сипаттау, бағыт беру, сылтау айту, амандасу: What's she like? · Turn left. · How are you?

ru.title: Описание
ru.html: What's he like? — характер (kind and funny). What does she look like? — внешность (tall with dark hair). What's it like? — It's big and modern.

kk.title: Сипаттау
kk.html: What's he like? — мінез (kind and funny). What does she look like? — сыртқы келбет (tall with dark hair). What's it like? — It's big and modern.

ru.title: Направления и приветствия
ru.html: Направления: Go straight on. Turn left/right. It's on your left. Приветствия: Hello/Hi. How are you? — Fine, thanks. Nice to meet you.

kk.title: Бағыт пен амандасу
kk.html: Бағыт: Go straight on. Turn left/right. It's on your left. Амандасу: Hello/Hi. How are you? — Fine, thanks. Nice to meet you.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Hi! How are you?» — «Fine, thanks. Sorry I'm late.» — «No worries. Turn right at the corner.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Hi! How are you?» — «Fine, thanks. Sorry I'm late.» — «No worries. Turn right at the corner.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: How is she like? → What's she like? What she looks like? → What does she look like? Go to straight → Go straight on.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: How is she like? → What's she like? What she looks like? → What does she look like? Go to straight → Go straight on.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: What's … like? (характер); What does … look like? (внешность). Направления: go straight, turn left/right. Приветствия: How are you? — Fine, thanks.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: What's … like? (мінез); What does … look like? (келбет). Бағыт: go straight, turn left/right. Амандасу: How are you? — Fine, thanks.

activities.q: You want to know someone's personality. You ask:

activities.prompt: Match each question to a good answer.
activities.why: like=character; look like=appearance; how are you=state.

why: Correct — mirror it: Nice to meet you too.

activities.after: your right, next to the café.

activities.scene: Meet someone and help them find a place.

steps.npc: Hi, nice to meet you! How do I get to the library?

options.t: Nice to meet you too. Go straight on and it's on your left.
How are you like? Go to straight.
options.why: “Nice to meet you too. Go straight on…”

activities.why: What's … like? (character), look like (appearance), go straight/turn left (directions), standard greetings.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Polite ways to ask for information, invite, offer and ask permission — mostly with Can/Could/Would.

Could you tell me …? · Would you like …? · Can I …?

learn.title: Asking for information
learn.html:
Could you tell me …?Could you tell me the time?
Do you know …?Do you know where the station is?
Excuse me, …?Excuse me, is there a bank near here?

learn.title: Invitations, offers, permission
learn.html:
invitationWould you like to come? · How about a coffee?
offerCan I help you? · Shall I open the window?
permissionCan/Could I use your phone? — Yes, of course.

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Excuse me, could you tell me the way to the museum?
Sure. Would you like me to show you?
That's kind. Can I follow you?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Would you like come?  ✓ Would you like to come?
✗
Can you tell me where is the station?  ✓ …where the station is. (indirect: normal order)
✗
I can use your phone? (asking)  ✓ Can I use your phone?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Information: Could you tell me…? Do you know…?
  • Invite: Would you like to…? Offer: Can I…? / Shall I…?
  • Permission: Can/Could I…? — Yes, of course.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Вежливые способы спросить информацию, пригласить, предложить и попросить разрешения — обычно с Can/Could/Would: Could you tell me…? · Would you like…? · Can I…?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Ақпарат сұраудың, шақырудың, ұсынудың және рұқсат сұраудың сыпайы жолдары — көбіне Can/Could/Would-пен: Could you tell me…? · Would you like…? · Can I…?

ru.title: Спросить информацию
ru.html: Could you tell me…? · Do you know…? · Excuse me, …? Напр.: Could you tell me the time? Do you know where the station is?

kk.title: Ақпарат сұрау
kk.html: Could you tell me…? · Do you know…? · Excuse me, …? Мыс.: Could you tell me the time? Do you know where the station is?

ru.title: Приглашение, предложение, разрешение
ru.html: Приглашение: Would you like to come? / How about a coffee? Предложение: Can I help? / Shall I…? Разрешение: Can/Could I…? — Yes, of course.

kk.title: Шақыру, ұсыныс, рұқсат
kk.html: Шақыру: Would you like to come? / How about a coffee? Ұсыныс: Can I help? / Shall I…? Рұқсат: Can/Could I…? — Yes, of course.

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Excuse me, could you tell me the way to the museum?» — «Sure. Would you like me to show you?»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Excuse me, could you tell me the way to the museum?» — «Sure. Would you like me to show you?»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Would you like come? → Would you like to come? where is the station → where the station is. I can use your phone? (прося) → Can I use your phone?

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Would you like come? → Would you like to come? where is the station → where the station is. I can use your phone? (сұрап) → Can I use your phone?

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Информация: Could you tell me…? Do you know…? Приглашение: Would you like to…? Предложение: Can I…? / Shall I…? Разрешение: Can/Could I…? — Yes, of course.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Ақпарат: Could you tell me…? Do you know…? Шақыру: Would you like to…? Ұсыныс: Can I…? / Shall I…? Рұқсат: Can/Could I…? — Yes, of course.

activities.q: You want to open the window. You ask:

items.s: Could you tell me the time?
Could you tell me what time is it?

activities.why: would like + to; indirect question uses normal word order.

activities.after: you like to join us for dinner?

activities.prompt: Match each phrase to its function.

pairs.l: Do you know where it is?

activities.before: Do you know where the station

activities.q: Someone is carrying heavy bags. You say:
why: That asks for help, not offers it.

activities.why: would like + to: “Would you like to come in?”

options.t: Thanks. Could I use your bathroom?
Thanks. I can use your bathroom?

steps.npc: Would you like something to drink?

options.t: Yes, please. A glass of water would be lovely.
Yes, I would like drink.

activities.why: Could you tell me…? (information), Would you like to…? (invite), Can/Shall I…? (offer), Can/Could I…? (permission).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Phrases for preferences, requests and suggestions.

I'd rather stay in. · Can you help? · Shall we go? · Let's eat.

learn.title: Preferences
learn.html:
I prefer … to …I prefer tea to coffee.
I'd rather + base verbI'd rather walk (than drive).
I'd prefer to …I'd prefer to stay home.

learn.title: Requests & suggestions
learn.html:
requestssuggestions
Can/Could you …? · Would you mind + -ing?Shall we …? · Let's … · How about + -ing? · Why don't we …?

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
Shall we go out tonight?
I'd rather stay in, honestly. How about watching a film?
Good idea. Could you make some popcorn?

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
I'd rather to stay.  ✓ I'd rather stay. (no 'to')
✗
How about to go?  ✓ How about going? (+ -ing)
✗
Would you mind to open …?  ✓ Would you mind opening …?

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Preferences: I prefer X to Y; I'd rather + base verb.
  • Requests: Can/Could you…? Would you mind + -ing?
  • Suggestions: Shall we…? Let's… How about + -ing?

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Фразы для предпочтений, просьб и предложений: I'd rather stay in. · Can you help? · Shall we go? · Let's eat.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Артық көру, өтініш және ұсыныс тіркестері: I'd rather stay in. · Can you help? · Shall we go? · Let's eat.

ru.title: Предпочтения
ru.html: I prefer … to … (I prefer tea to coffee). I'd rather + глагол (I'd rather walk). I'd prefer to … (stay home).

kk.title: Артық көру
kk.html: I prefer … to … (I prefer tea to coffee). I'd rather + етістік (I'd rather walk). I'd prefer to … (stay home).

ru.title: Просьбы и предложения
ru.html: Просьба: Can/Could you…? · Would you mind + -ing? Предложение: Shall we…? · Let's… · How about + -ing? · Why don't we…?

kk.title: Өтініш пен ұсыныс
kk.html: Өтініш: Can/Could you…? · Would you mind + -ing? Ұсыныс: Shall we…? · Let's… · How about + -ing? · Why don't we…?

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «Shall we go out tonight?» — «I'd rather stay in. How about watching a film?» — «Could you make popcorn?»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «Shall we go out tonight?» — «I'd rather stay in. How about watching a film?» — «Could you make popcorn?»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: I'd rather to stay → I'd rather stay. How about to go? → How about going? mind to open → mind opening.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: I'd rather to stay → I'd rather stay. How about to go? → How about going? mind to open → mind opening.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Предпочтения: I prefer X to Y; I'd rather + глагол. Просьба: Can/Could you…? Would you mind + -ing? Предложение: Shall we…? Let's… How about + -ing?

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Артық көру: I prefer X to Y; I'd rather + етістік. Өтініш: Can/Could you…? Would you mind + -ing? Ұсыныс: Shall we…? Let's… How about + -ing?

options: Shall we go to the park?
We are going to the park.

activities.prompt: Match each phrase to its function.

activities.q: “Cinema or a walk?” — “___” (you want the walk)
options: I'd rather go for a walk.

options.t: I'd rather cook at home. How about making pasta?
I'd rather to cook. How about to make pasta?

activities.why: Preferences (I'd rather + base, prefer X to Y), requests (Could you…?), suggestions (Shall we…? How about + -ing?).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Days, months and dates use capital letters and specific prepositions: on for days/dates, in for months/years.

on Monday · in July · on the 3rd of May

learn.title: Prepositions of date
learn.html:
on + day / dateon Friday, on 1 June, on my birthday
in + month / year / seasonin April, in 2025, in winter
at + festival / weekendat Christmas, at the weekend

learn.title: Saying dates
learn.html: Write 3 May / May 3; say the third of May / May the third. Dates use ordinals (first, second, third…).

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
When's the meeting?
On Tuesday, the fifteenth of March.
And the holidays?
In July, at the weekend.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
in Monday  ✓ on Monday.
✗
on July  ✓ in July.
✗
monday, july  ✓ Monday, July (capital letters)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• on + day/date; in + month/year/season; at + weekend/festival.
  • Days and months take capital letters.
  • Dates use ordinals: the third of May.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Дни, месяцы и даты пишутся с заглавной буквы и с определёнными предлогами: on — день/дата, in — месяц/год: on Monday · in July.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Күндер, айлар, даталар бас әріппен жазылады және белгілі предлогтармен: on — күн/дата, in — ай/жыл: on Monday · in July.

ru.title: Предлоги даты
ru.html: on + день/дата (on Friday, on 1 June). in + месяц/год/сезон (in April, in 2025, in winter). at + праздник/выходные (at Christmas, at the weekend).

kk.title: Дата предлогтары
kk.html: on + күн/дата (on Friday, on 1 June). in + ай/жыл/маусым (in April, in 2025, in winter). at + мереке/демалыс (at Christmas, at the weekend).

ru.title: Как называть даты
ru.html: Пишем: 3 May / May 3; говорим: the third of May. Даты — порядковыми (first, second, third…).

kk.title: Датаны айту
kk.html: Жазу: 3 May / May 3; айту: the third of May. Даталар реттік санмен (first, second, third…).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «When's the meeting?» — «On Tuesday, the fifteenth of March.» «The holidays?» — «In July, at the weekend.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «When's the meeting?» — «On Tuesday, the fifteenth of March.» «The holidays?» — «In July, at the weekend.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: in Monday → on Monday. on July → in July. monday, july → Monday, July.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: in Monday → on Monday. on July → in July. monday, july → Monday, July.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: on + день/дата; in + месяц/год/сезон; at + выходные/праздник. Дни и месяцы с заглавной. Даты порядковыми.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: on + күн/дата; in + ай/жыл/маусым; at + демалыс/мереке. Күндер мен айлар бас әріппен. Даталар реттік санмен.

activities.why: on days/dates; in months/years; at weekend/festivals.

options: We met on tuesday in april.
We met on Tuesday in April.

options.t: On the tenth of August, in the morning.
options.why: on + date; in + part of day.

options.t: In the ten of August, on the morning.
options.why: “On the tenth of August, in the morning.”

activities.why: on + day/date, in + month/year/season, at + weekend/festival; days and months are capitalised.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
We tell the time with past and to, and use at for clock times.

It's half past six. · It's quarter to nine. · See you at 7.

learn.title: Telling the time
learn.html:
3:00three o'clock
3:15(a) quarter past three
3:30half past three
3:45(a) quarter to four
3:20 / 3:50twenty past three / ten to four

learn.title: Prepositions of time
learn.html:
at + clock time / nightat 6 o'clock, at midnight, at night
in + part of the dayin the morning/afternoon/evening
on + day + parton Monday morning

learn.title: Everyday conversation
learn.html:
What time does the film start?
At quarter past eight.
Let's meet at eight, in the evening.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
at the morning  ✓ in the morning.
✗
in 6 o'clock  ✓ at 6 o'clock.
✗
It's half to seven.  ✓ It's half past six. (half PAST)

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• past (after the hour) / to (before the next hour).
  • at + clock time & night; in + part of the day.
  • quarter past, half past, quarter to.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Время называем через past и to, а для времени по часам — at: It's half past six. · It's quarter to nine. · See you at 7.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Уақытты past пен to арқылы айтамыз, сағат уақытына at қолданамыз: It's half past six. · It's quarter to nine. · See you at 7.

ru.title: Как называть время
ru.html: 3:00 three o'clock; 3:15 quarter past three; 3:30 half past three; 3:45 quarter to four; 3:20 twenty past three; 3:50 ten to four.

kk.title: Уақытты айту
kk.html: 3:00 three o'clock; 3:15 quarter past three; 3:30 half past three; 3:45 quarter to four; 3:20 twenty past three; 3:50 ten to four.

ru.title: Предлоги времени
ru.html: at + время/ночь (at 6 o'clock, at night). in + часть суток (in the morning). on + день + часть (on Monday morning).

kk.title: Уақыт предлогтары
kk.html: at + сағат/түн (at 6 o'clock, at night). in + тәулік бөлігі (in the morning). on + күн + бөлік (on Monday morning).

ru.title: Повседневный разговор
ru.html: «What time does the film start?» — «At quarter past eight.» — «Let's meet at eight, in the evening.»

kk.title: Күнделікті сөйлесу
kk.html: «What time does the film start?» — «At quarter past eight.» — «Let's meet at eight, in the evening.»

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: at the morning → in the morning. in 6 o'clock → at 6 o'clock. half to seven → half past six.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: at the morning → in the morning. in 6 o'clock → at 6 o'clock. half to seven → half past six.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: past (после часа) / to (до следующего часа). at + время/ночь; in + часть суток. quarter past, half past, quarter to.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: past (сағаттан кейін) / to (келесі сағатқа дейін). at + сағат/түн; in + тәулік бөлігі. quarter past, half past, quarter to.

why: We say half PAST, not half to.

activities.why: at + clock time; in + part of the day.

activities.prompt: Match each clock time to its phrase.
activities.why: past / half past / quarter to / o'clock.
part of the day → in the afternoon.

why: 'to' is used before the next hour.
Correct — after the hour → ten past seven.

options.t: At half past ten, in the morning.
options.why: at + time; in + part of day.

options.t: In half to ten, at the morning.

activities.why: Tell the time with past/to and quarter/half; use at for clock times, in for parts of the day.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Punctuation makes writing clear. The basics: capital letters, full stops (.), commas (,), question marks (?) and apostrophes (').

It's Monday. Do you like London, Paris and Rome?

learn.title: Capital letters
learn.html:
start of a sentenceThe sun is out.
the word IMy friend and I …
names, days, months, countriesAnna, Monday, July, France

learn.title: Full stops, commas, apostrophes
learn.html:
full stopends a statement: I'm home.
comma in a listapples, pears, and bananas
apostrophecontractions (don't) & possession (Anna's)

learn.title: See it in use
learn.html:
whats your name and where are you from
→ What's your name, and where are you from?
Much clearer!

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
i live in london.  ✓ I live in London.
✗
Do you like it  ✓ Do you like it?
✗
Its raining. / Its colour  ✓ It's raining. / Its colour.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Capitals: sentence start, I, names, days, months, countries.
  • . ends a statement; ? ends a question.
  • Apostrophe: contractions and possession.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Пунктуация делает текст ясным. Основы: заглавные буквы, точка (.), запятая (,), вопросительный знак (?), апостроф ('): It's Monday. Do you like London, Paris and Rome?

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Тыныс белгілері жазуды түсінікті етеді. Негіздер: бас әріп, нүкте (.), үтір (,), сұрақ белгісі (?), апостроф ('): It's Monday. Do you like London, Paris and Rome?

ru.title: Заглавные буквы
ru.html: Начало предложения (The sun…), слово I, имена/дни/месяцы/страны (Anna, Monday, July, France).

kk.title: Бас әріптер
kk.html: Сөйлем басы (The sun…), I сөзі, есімдер/күндер/айлар/елдер (Anna, Monday, July, France).

ru.title: Точка, запятая, апостроф
ru.html: Точка — конец утверждения. Запятая — в перечислении (apples, pears, and bananas). Апостроф — сокращения (don't) и принадлежность (Anna's).

kk.title: Нүкте, үтір, апостроф
kk.html: Нүкте — хабарлы сөйлемді аяқтайды. Үтір — тізімде (apples, pears, and bananas). Апостроф — қысқарту (don't) және иелік (Anna's).

ru.title: На практике
ru.html: «whats your name and where are you from» → What's your name, and where are you from?

kk.title: Қолданыстан
kk.html: «whats your name and where are you from» → What's your name, and where are you from?

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: i live in london → I live in London. Do you like it → …it? Its raining → It's raining.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: i live in london → I live in London. Do you like it → …it? Its raining → It's raining.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Заглавные: начало предложения, I, имена, дни, месяцы, страны. . завершает утверждение, ? — вопрос. Апостроф: сокращения и принадлежность.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Бас әріп: сөйлем басы, I, есімдер, күндер, айлар, елдер. . хабарлы сөйлемді, ? сұрақты аяқтайды. Апостроф: қысқарту мен иелік.

activities.why: Questions end with ?; it's = it is.

activities.prompt: my friend anna lives in france.
activities.answer: my friend anna lives in france.
alts: My friend Anna lives in France.
activities.why: Capitalise the start, names and countries: “My friend Anna lives in France.”

options: I bought bread milk and eggs.
I bought bread, milk and eggs.

steps.npc: 'i live in paris and i love it' — is that OK?

options.t: Almost — capital I, capital Paris, and a full stop.
options.why: I, Paris capitalised; end with a full stop.

options.t: Yes, that's perfect.
options.why: It needs capitals (I, Paris) and a full stop.

activities.why: Capitalise sentence starts, I, names, days, months and countries; end statements with . and questions with ?

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
When we add endings (-s, -ed, -ing) to a word, the spelling sometimes changes: we drop e, double a letter or change y → i.

make → making · stop → stopped · study → studies

learn.title: Drop the e
learn.html:
-e + ing → drop emake → making, write → writing
-e + ed → just add dlike → liked, hope → hoped

learn.title: Double the consonant
learn.html:
One short vowel + one consonant (in a stressed syllable) → double the last consonant.

stop → stopping/edrun → running
big → biggerbegin → beginning

learn.title: y → i
learn.html:
consonant + y → -ies/-iedstudy → studies/studied, try → tries/tried
vowel + y → no changeplay → plays/played, enjoy → enjoyed
keep y before -ingstudy → studying (not 'studiing')

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
makeing  ✓ making.
✗
stoped  ✓ stopped.
✗
studyed / studiing  ✓ studied / studying.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• -e + ing → drop the e (make→making).
  • short vowel + consonant → double (stop→stopped).
  • consonant + y → -ies/-ied; keep y before -ing.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: При добавлении -s, -ed, -ing написание иногда меняется: убираем e, удваиваем согласную или y → i: make → making · stop → stopped · study → studies.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: -s, -ed, -ing жалғауларын қосқанда емле кейде өзгереді: e түсеміз, дауыссызды екілендіреміз немесе y → i: make → making · stop → stopped · study → studies.

ru.title: Убрать e
ru.html: -e + ing → e убирается (make → making). -e + ed → только d (like → liked).

kk.title: e-ні түсіру
kk.html: -e + ing → e түседі (make → making). -e + ed → тек d (like → liked).

ru.title: Удвоение согласной
ru.html: Один краткий гласный + одна согласная (в ударном слоге) → удваиваем: stop → stopping, run → running, big → bigger.

kk.title: Дауыссызды екілендіру
kk.html: Бір қысқа дауысты + бір дауыссыз (екпінді буында) → соңғы дауыссызды екілендіру: stop → stopping, run → running, big → bigger.

ru.title: y → i
ru.html: согласный + y → -ies/-ied (study → studies/studied). гласный + y → без изменения (play → played). Перед -ing y сохраняется (studying).

kk.title: y → i
kk.html: дауыссыз + y → -ies/-ied (study → studies/studied). дауысты + y → өзгермейді (play → played). -ing алдында y сақталады (studying).

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: makeing → making. stoped → stopped. studyed/studiing → studied/studying.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: makeing → making. stoped → stopped. studyed/studiing → studied/studying.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: -e + ing → убираем e. краткий гласный + согласная → удвоение. согласный + y → -ies/-ied; перед -ing y сохраняется.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: -e + ing → e түседі. қысқа дауысты + дауыссыз → екілену. дауыссыз + y → -ies/-ied; -ing алдында y сақталады.

why: The stress isn't on the last syllable, so no doubling.

steps.npc: I wrote: 'I am studing and runing every day.'

options.t: Small fixes: studying and running.
That looks fine to me.

activities.why: Drop e before -ing, double the consonant after a short vowel, and change y→i (but keep y before -ing).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some spelling patterns cause trouble: ie / ei, silent letters, and plurals like -o → -oes.

believe · receive · know · potatoes

learn.title: i before e
learn.html:
"i before e, except after c": believe, friend, piece — but receive, ceiling (after c).

A few exceptions: their, weird, eight.

learn.title: Silent letters
learn.html:
silent kknow, knife, knee
silent wwrite, wrong, who
silent b / hclimb, hour, honest

learn.title: Plurals: -o and -f
learn.html: Some nouns ending in -o add -es: potato → potatoes, tomato → tomatoes. Many -f/-fe → -ves: leaf → leaves, wife → wives.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
freind / recieve  ✓ friend / receive.
✗
potatos / tomatos  ✓ potatoes / tomatoes.
✗
nife / no (know)  ✓ knife / know.

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• i before e, except after c (believe / receive).
  • Silent letters: know, write, hour, climb.
  • potato→potatoes; leaf→leaves.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые модели написания сложны: ie / ei, немые буквы, множ. -o → -oes: believe · receive · know · potatoes.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір емле үлгілері қиын: ie / ei, үнсіз әріптер, -o → -oes көпшесі: believe · receive · know · potatoes.

ru.title: i before e
ru.html: «i перед e, кроме как после c»: believe, friend, piece — но receive, ceiling (после c). Исключения: their, weird, eight.

kk.title: i before e
kk.html: «i before e, except after c»: believe, friend, piece — бірақ receive, ceiling (c-дан кейін). Ерекшеліктер: their, weird, eight.

ru.title: Немые буквы
ru.html: немая k: know, knife, knee. немая w: write, wrong, who. немая b/h: climb, hour, honest.

kk.title: Үнсіз әріптер
kk.html: үнсіз k: know, knife, knee. үнсіз w: write, wrong, who. үнсіз b/h: climb, hour, honest.

ru.title: Множ.: -o и -f
ru.html: Некоторые -o + -es: potato → potatoes, tomato → tomatoes. Многие -f/-fe → -ves: leaf → leaves, wife → wives.

kk.title: Көпше: -o және -f
kk.html: -o кейбіреуі -es: potato → potatoes, tomato → tomatoes. Көп -f/-fe → -ves: leaf → leaves, wife → wives.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: freind/recieve → friend/receive. potatos/tomatos → potatoes/tomatoes. nife/no → knife/know.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: freind/recieve → friend/receive. potatos/tomatos → potatoes/tomatoes. nife/no → knife/know.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: i before e, после c — ei (believe/receive). Немые буквы: know, write, hour, climb. potato→potatoes; leaf→leaves.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: i before e, c-дан кейін ei (believe/receive). Үнсіз әріптер: know, write, hour, climb. potato→potatoes; leaf→leaves.

activities.q: Which word has a silent letter?

activities.prompt: Match each word to its silent letter.

options.t: 'Friend' — i before e.
'Freind' looks right.

activities.why: i before e except after c; watch silent letters and -o/-f plurals (potatoes, leaves).

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
Some very common words are often misspelled. It's worth learning these by heart.

because · necessary · beautiful · different · tomorrow

learn.title: Tricky common words
learn.html:
becausenot 'becuase'
beautifulnot 'beatiful'
necessaryone c, two s
differenttwo f's
tomorrowone m, two r's

learn.title: Words people confuse
learn.html:
this…not that
their / there / they're(possessive / place / they are)
your / you're(possessive / you are)
its / it's(possessive / it is)

learn.title: Memory tricks
learn.html: because = "Big Elephants Can Always Understand Small Elephants". necessary = one Coffee, two Sugars (1 c, 2 s).

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
Their going home.  ✓ They're going home.
✗
Your late.  ✓ You're late.
✗
alot  ✓ a lot (two words).

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Learn tricky words: because, necessary, beautiful, different.
  • their/there/they're; your/you're; its/it's.
  • 'a lot' is two words.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Некоторые частые слова часто пишут с ошибкой. Их стоит выучить: because · necessary · beautiful · different · tomorrow.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Кейбір өте жиі сөздер қате жазылады. Оларды жаттап алған дұрыс: because · necessary · beautiful · different · tomorrow.

ru.title: Сложные частые слова
ru.html: because (не becuase), beautiful (не beatiful), necessary (одна c, две s), different (две f), tomorrow (одна m, две r).

kk.title: Қиын жиі сөздер
kk.html: because (becuase емес), beautiful (beatiful емес), necessary (бір c, екі s), different (екі f), tomorrow (бір m, екі r).

ru.title: Путают эти слова
ru.html: their (принадл.) / there (место) / they're (they are). your (принадл.) / you're (you are). its (принадл.) / it's (it is).

kk.title: Шатастыратын сөздер
kk.html: their (иелік) / there (орын) / they're (they are). your (иелік) / you're (you are). its (иелік) / it's (it is).

ru.title: Приёмы запоминания
ru.html: necessary = один Coffee, два Sugars (1 c, 2 s). Читайте по слогам: to-mor-row.

kk.title: Есте сақтау айласы
kk.html: necessary = бір Coffee, екі Sugars (1 c, 2 s). Сөздерді дауыстап буындап оқыңыз: to-mor-row.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: Their going → They're going. Your late → You're late. alot → a lot.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: Their going → They're going. Your late → You're late. alot → a lot.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Учите сложные слова: because, necessary, beautiful, different. their/there/they're; your/you're; its/it's. 'a lot' — два слова.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Қиын сөздерді жаттаңыз: because, necessary, beautiful, different. their/there/they're; your/you're; its/it's. 'a lot' — екі сөз.

activities.prompt: Match each word to its meaning.

steps.npc: 'Your the best, and their coming tomorow!'

options.t: Fixes: You're, they're, tomorrow.
options.why: you're / they're / tomorrow (one m, two r).

options.t: Looks perfect!

activities.why: Memorise tricky words (because, necessary) and don't confuse their/there/they're, your/you're, its/it's.

learn.title: What is this grammar?
learn.html:
We build new words with prefixes (before), suffixes (after), and by joining words into compounds.

unhappy · careful · sunglasses

learn.title: Prefixes (change meaning)
learn.html:
un- / in- / im- = notunhappy, incorrect, impossible
re- = againredo, rewrite
dis- = oppositedisagree, dislike

learn.title: Suffixes (change word type)
learn.html:
-ful / -lesscareful (with care) / careless (without)
-er / -or = personteacher, actor, driver
-ly = adverbquick → quickly
-ness = nounhappy → happiness

learn.title: Compound words
learn.html: Two words → one: sun + glasses = sunglasses; tooth + brush = toothbrush; bed + room = bedroom.

learn.title: Common mistakes
learn.html:
✗
unpossible  ✓ impossible.
✗
carefull  ✓ careful (one l).
✗
happyness  ✓ happiness (y → i).

learn.title: Quick summary
learn.html:
• Prefixes change meaning: un-, in-, im-, re-, dis-.
  • Suffixes change word type: -ful, -less, -er, -ly, -ness.
  • Compounds join two words: sunglasses, bedroom.

ru.title: Что это за грамматика?
ru.html: Новые слова строим с помощью приставок (спереди), суффиксов (сзади) и сложения слов: unhappy · careful · sunglasses.

kk.title: Бұл қандай грамматика?
kk.html: Жаңа сөздерді префикс (алдында), суффикс (соңында) және сөздерді біріктіру арқылы жасаймыз: unhappy · careful · sunglasses.

ru.title: Приставки (меняют смысл)
ru.html: un-/in-/im- = не (unhappy, incorrect, impossible). re- = снова (redo, rewrite). dis- = противоположность (disagree, dislike).

kk.title: Префикстер (мағынаны өзгертеді)
kk.html: un-/in-/im- = емес (unhappy, incorrect, impossible). re- = қайта (redo, rewrite). dis- = кері (disagree, dislike).

ru.title: Суффиксы (меняют часть речи)
ru.html: -ful/-less (careful/careless). -er/-or = человек (teacher, actor). -ly = наречие (quickly). -ness = сущ. (happiness).

kk.title: Суффикстер (сөз түрін өзгертеді)
kk.html: -ful/-less (careful/careless). -er/-or = адам (teacher, actor). -ly = үстеу (quickly). -ness = зат есім (happiness).

ru.title: Сложные слова
ru.html: Два слова → одно: sun + glasses = sunglasses; tooth + brush = toothbrush; bed + room = bedroom.

kk.title: Біріккен сөздер
kk.html: Екі сөз → бір: sun + glasses = sunglasses; tooth + brush = toothbrush; bed + room = bedroom.

ru.title: Частые ошибки
ru.html: unpossible → impossible. carefull → careful. happyness → happiness.

kk.title: Жиі кездесетін қателер
kk.html: unpossible → impossible. carefull → careful. happyness → happiness.

ru.title: Краткий итог
ru.html: Приставки меняют смысл: un-, in-, im-, re-, dis-. Суффиксы меняют часть речи: -ful, -less, -er, -ly, -ness. Сложные слова: sunglasses, bedroom.

kk.title: Қысқаша қорытынды
kk.html: Префикстер мағынаны өзгертеді: un-, in-, im-, re-, dis-. Суффикстер сөз түрін өзгертеді: -ful, -less, -er, -ly, -ness. Біріккен сөздер: sunglasses, bedroom.

activities.why: im- before p; careful has one l.

An actder.
Dislike — dis + like.
Unlike-hood.

activities.why: Prefixes change meaning (un-, im-, re-, dis-), suffixes change word type (-ful, -er, -ness), and compounds join two words.
